03.04.2025 12
MANUFACTURING/SALE OF FAKE FOOD PRODUCTS
*461. SHRIMATI MALVIKA DEVI:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure that the
organic way of growing vegetables is encouraged and used by the industries to maintain
food quality;
(b) the details of the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure that the exact
amount of sugar content is mentioned in processed foods along with the type of sugar
present in the specific product;
(c) the details of the steps taken by the Government against the companies that are
manufacturing fake products similar to the actual products made by big companies and
sold in rural areas and small villages; and
(d) the details of the punitive action taken by the Government along with the number of
companies penalized in this regard during the last year and the current year?
THE MINISTER OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES (SHRI CHIRAG PASWAN):
(a) to (d): A Statement is laid on the Table of the House.
STATEMENT
(a): Government is promoting organic farming and growing all agricultural and horticulture
crops including vegetables, through the schemes of Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana
(PKVY) in all the States/UTs (except North Eastern States), whereas for North Eastern
States, Mission Organic Value Chain Development for North Eastern Region
03.04.2025 13
(MOVCDNER) scheme is being implemented. Both the schemes stress on end-to-end
support to farmers engaged in organic farming i.e. from production to post-harvest
management training and capacity building. The main focus of the PKVY and
MOVCDNER scheme is to promote natural resource based integrated and climate
resilient sustainable farming systems that ensure maintenance and increase of soil
fertility, natural resource conservation, on-farm nutrient recycling and minimize
dependence of farmers on external inputs.
To promote marketing of organic produce, two types of organic certifications
systems have been developed to ensure quality control of organic produce, as given
below:
(i) Third Party Certification by Accredited Certification Agency under National Programme
for Organic Production (NPOP) scheme under Ministry of Commerce and Industry for
development of export market. Under NPOP certification scheme the production and
handling of activities at all stages such as production, processing, trading and export
requirements for organic products is covered.
(ii) Participatory Guarantee System (PGS-India) under Ministry of Agriculture and
Farmers Welfare in which stakeholders (including farmers/ producers) are involved in
decision making about the operation of the PGS-India certification itself by assessing,
inspecting and verifying the production practices of each other. PGS- India certification is
to meet the demand of domestic market.
03.04.2025 14
Further, Food safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), a statutory body
under the administration of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, has notified the
Food Safety and Standards (Organic Foods) Regulations, 2017 regarding manufacture,
pack, sell, offer for sale, market or otherwise distribute or import of Organic food. As per
this regulation, all foods offered for sale as 'Organic Food' has to comply with the
provisions of National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) or Participatory
Guarantee System for India (PGS-India).
Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI),
implemented by the Ministry of Food Processing Industries, specify that the entire
manufacturing process, including primary processing, of the food products eligible for
coverage under the scheme, must occur in India, with the exception of additives, flavors,
and edible oils. This approach aims to benefit farmers by procurement of domestically
grown agricultural products in the manufacturing process thereby increasing their income.
The Category II component of the scheme covers Innovative / Organic products including
Free Range – Eggs, Poultry, Meat and Egg Products. 16 proposals of Organic products
under Category II have been approved under the PLISFPI and an incentive of Rs. 4.24
crore has been disbursed.
(b) to (d): Food Safety and Standard Act 2006 and Rules and Regulations framed
thereunder, lay down the quality and safety standards for various articles of food. The
issues related to food safety, adulteration, labelling violations, Quality control, inspections
and penalties for violation of Food Safety and Standards Act 2006, Rules and
03.04.2025 15
Regulations, are dealt by Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) under the
administrative control of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. Implementation and
enforcement of these standards lies with the FSSAI and State/UT Governments.
FSSAI has notified “Food Safety and Standards (Labelling and Display)
Regulations, 2020” and this regulation mandates the mention of nutritional information on
the labels with respect to salt, sugar and fat. It also mandates mandatory declarations of
the name of sweetener with purity and weight percent of marker compound. Further,
FSSAI has published the draft Food Safety and Standards (Labelling and Display)
Amendment Regulations, 2025 that mandates the mention of nutritional information on
the labels with respect to salt, sugar and fat in bold letters and relatively increased font
size on labels.
FSSAI undertakes regular surveillance, monitoring, inspection and random
sampling of food products through its Regional Offices and State Food Safety
Departments throughout the year in urban and rural areas.
In case of non-conformity found, measures are taken as per FSS Act and Rules &
Regulations made thereunder. Chapter IX (Offences and Penalties) Section 48-67
describe the penalties for the production, sale, storage etc. of food which is in
contravention of the FSS Act, 2006, the rules and regulations thereunder. Punitive action
includes penalties and criminal prosecution in cases of unsafe food.
03.04.2025 16
As informed by Food Safety and Standards Authority of India, the details of
samples analysed and action taken including penalties imposed and convictions made,
during last two years, is given below:
No. of No. of samples Non-Conforming samples Civil Cases Criminal Cases
Year samples found
Analysed nonconforming Unsafe Sub- Labelling No. of Penalties No. of Penalties
Standard defects/ convictions Raised convictions Raised
Misleading (Rs. In (Rs. In
/ Misc. Crores) Crores)

24
SHRIMATI MALVIKA DEVI: Thank you, Speaker Sir.
Thank you, our hon. Prime Minister and our hon. Minister of Food Processing
Industries Shri Chirag Paswan Ji for giving such a detailed answer.
Through you, Speaker Sir, I would like to ask the Minister what steps are being
taken to promote and encourage organic processed food products.
SHRI CHIRAG PASWAN: Sir, through you, I would like to inform the hon. Member that
organic food products are those products in which the input has been organic, that has
been processed from the ingredients by using the organic farming methods. …
(Interruptions)
To promote the organic farming, I am very happy and very proud to share this that
in the past one decade under the leadership and the guidance of our Prime Minister, the
processing and the farming of the organic food process have been made more organized
03.04.2025 17
and a lot of schemes through various other Ministries has been floated to enhance
organic farming. … (Interruptions)
Through these schemes, even through the Agriculture Ministry, they have come up
with two new schemes that were started in the year 2015-16. Paramparagat Krishi Vikas
Yojana was there in all the States except the North- Eastern States and there was the
separate scheme for the North-Eastern States and that scheme was for the North-
Eastern States. Mission Organic Value Chain Development (MOVCD) for the North
Eastern Region. … (Interruptions)
So, through these schemes, end to end support to the farmers, who are engaged
in organic farming, was provided right from the production to processing, certification to
marketing to post-harvest management training was provided. … (Interruptions)
I am very happy to share the data, through you, to the hon. Member that under
these schemes, cumulative of Rs. 3674.84 crore has been released, which has benefited
around 27.4 lakh farmers across the country.
Sir, in my Ministry, through PMKSY, PMFME, and PLI Schemes, we are promoting
organic farming, especially for organic food products… (Interruptions) We have created a
dedicated category for innovation and organic food products under the PLI scheme…
(Interruptions)
SHRIMATI MALVIKA DEVI: What mechanisms have been put in place by the Ministry to
curb the sale of counterfeit, spurious, and adulterated products? … (Interruptions)
SHRI CHIRAG PASWAN: Sir, first of all, I am surprised that when such important
03.04.2025 18
questions are being asked concerning the consumers और अ(cid:198)नदाताओ ंको लेकर जब ये सवाल
पूछे जाते ह(cid:167) तो िवप(cid:177) का यह रवैया कभी-कभी िचंताजनक हो जाता है । (cid:255)(cid:307) काल के व(cid:265) जब िवप(cid:177) ऐसे करता
है तो देश क(cid:236) जनता देखती है िक िकसान(cid:332) और कं (cid:186)यूमस(cid:170) को लेकर ये िकतने िचंितत ह(cid:167) ।… (Interruptions)
11.06 hrs
At this stage, Shri Kodikunnil Suresh, Shrimati Supriya Sule and some other
hon. Members left the House.
Coming back to the question that was asked by the hon. Member, while curbing
the sale of counterfeit and spurious adulterated food products, to be very honest, it does
not directly come under the mandate of my Ministry. However, the Government of India,
through various other Ministries, including mine, has a comprehensive legal and
institutional framework to address such issues, through the Consumer Protection Act, the
Trade Marks Act, and the Food Safety and Standards Act of 2006, Bureau of Indian
Standards Act of 2016 as well as the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita of 2023.
Through these frameworks, we try to control all the adulteration that is happening.
They are the regulatory bodies that make regulations for this. This robust network of food
testing laboratories notified by FSSAI plays a very critical role. We also support them
through PMKSY and we provide these food testing labs across the country.
(cid:174)ी नवीन िजंदल : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, भारत सरकार (cid:300)ारा फे क (cid:255)ोड(cid:179)ट्स को रेगुलेट करन े के िलए देशभर म(cid:164)

सर, आप जानते ह(cid:167) िक आजकल खाने के अंदर कई बार बह(cid:242)त अिधक पेि(cid:214)टसाइड, िविडसाइड और

का काय(cid:170) करती है । इस बात क(cid:236) िनरतं रता म(cid:164), through all the possible regulatory bodies इस बात को
हम लोग सिु नि(cid:306)त करने का (cid:255)यास करते ह(cid:167) िक फू ड (cid:255)ोड(cid:179)ट्स क(cid:236) गणु व(cid:176)ा को लेकर िकसी तरीके का कोई भी

ह(cid:167)?
THE MINISTER OF HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS; AND MINISTER OF POWER
(SHRI MANOHAR LAL):
(a) to (f): A Statement is laid on the Table of the House.
STATEMENT
(a) : Street Lighting National Programme (SLNP), launched in 2015, aims to reduce
energy consumption and costs in public lighting through the widespread adoption of LED
street lighting across India. Energy Efficiency Services Limited (EESL), a joint venture of
CPSEs under the Ministry of Power is the implementing agency for SLNP.
03.04.2025 21
More than 13.1 million LED streetlights have been installed across different urban
and rural areas under SLNP. This has resulted in an annual electricity energy saving,
approximately 8.8. billion kWh, and annual monetary savings of around ₹6,178 crore for
municipalities and gram panchayats. The State-wise details of LED streetlights installed is
given at Annexure.
(b) and (c): SLNP has been implemented in 69 State bodies/ Authorities including 51
Nos of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in Uttar Pradesh. The Scheme has not yet been
implemented in Baghpat district.
(d) : Government of India has not allocated any budget for SLNP, as the programme is
implemented by EESL in self-financing mode.
(e) and (f) : There have been generally no delays at EESL end in implementation of the
SLNP scheme once the agreements and consents from the respective authority is
finalised.
However, the outstanding receivables against SLNP amounting to approximately
₹2700.54 crores {excluding Delayed Payment Surcharge (DPS)}, as on 31/03/25 from
various States and ULBs, is one of the major challenges being faced by the company
owing to implementation of the programme. EESL and Ministry of Power are rigorously
following up with the States/UTs concerned for expediting the release of EESL’s
outstanding payment towards SLNP.
03.04.2025 22
SLNP has largely achieved its objective of catalysing the adoption of energy
efficient street lights in the country and now urban and rural local bodies are taking it
forward through alternative options including CAPEX model.
ANNEXURE
Since FY 2014-15, EESL has installed Street Lights in the below-mentioned
States/UTs and the State-wise installation details as on 31.03.2025 are as under:

से (cid:255)(cid:307) है िक (cid:179)या सांसद िनिध से इन सोलर लाइट्स के िलए वन-फोथ(cid:170) पैसा हम द(cid:164) और तीन-चौथाई पैसा के (cid:198)(cid:254)
सरकार देकर इस योजना को (cid:179)या पुन: चालू करग(cid:164) े? इसके अलावा जो लाइट्स ऑलरडे ी लगी ह(cid:242)ई ह(cid:167), उनके
सोलर पैन(cid:208)स और पो(cid:208)स ऑलरडे ी लगे ह(cid:242)ए ह(cid:167), के वल बैटरी च(cid:167)ज करनी है । अगर आप बैटरी च(cid:167)ज करवा द(cid:164)गे तो
सभी सांसद(cid:332) का एक बह(cid:242)त बड़ा काम हो जाएगा और हमारी सोलर लाइट्स भी अ(cid:184)छे से चल(cid:164)गी । मं(cid:253)ी जी (cid:179)या
इस िवषय म(cid:164) कुछ करना चाह(cid:164)गे?
(cid:174)ी मनोहर लाल : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, हमारी िकसी (cid:255)कार क(cid:236) कोई कं िडशन नह(cid:233) है िक िकस मद से िकतना पैसा

चयन कर सकता है या जहां सात साल पूरे हो गए ह(cid:167), उन (cid:214)थान(cid:332) पर ए(cid:179)सट(cid:164)ड भी कर सकता है । यह योजना
कह(cid:233) (cid:321)क(cid:236) नह(cid:233) है ।
माननीय अ(cid:197) य(cid:177) : (cid:179) वेशचन न(cid:204) बर - 463, (cid:174)ी (cid:174)ीरगं आ(cid:200) पा चंदू बारणे।
CLEAN ENERGY FOR ECONOMIC GROWTH
*463. †SHRI SHRIRANG APPA CHANDU BARNE:
SHRIMATI BHARTI PARDHI:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) whether clean energy is projected to be a key factor in India's economic
development and in addressing the needs of its population, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has been pitching for clean energy cooking fuel innovations
and extended open invitation to the entrepreneurs for the said purpose;
(c) if so, the details thereof;
(d) the details of the existing gas-based economy of the country as on date along with
the details of targets, if any, fixed by the Government for its future expansion;
(e) the details of corrective steps taken/being taken by the Government for developing
nation wide gas grid and other infrastructure required for achieving such targets; and
(f) the details of expenditure likely to be incurred for developing such national assets
along with the details of funds allocated and released so far by the Government in this
regard?
03.04.2025 27
THE MINISTER OF PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS (SHRI HARDEEP SINGH
PURI):
(a) to (f): A Statement is laid on the Table of the House
STATEMENT
(a) :Yes, Clean energy occupies a pivotal place in Government’s efforts for meeting
increasing energy demand. As per Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy
2024, India currently consumes about 6.3% of World Primary Energy (2023) which would
reach 12.5% of the World’s Primary Energy by 2050. Further, as per BP Energy Outlook,
2023, Renewable energy consumption will grow at an average of 4-6% a year in India
and it will become the major source of primary energy for India in 2050.
India’s energy demand is increasing continuously due to sustained economic
growth over the last many years. India currently has an average GDP growth rate of 6.5%
with a USD 4 trillion economy. As per the IEA’s World Energy Outlook, 2024, India will
have the highest increase in energy demand than any other country over the next decade
mainly because of its size and the scale of rising demand from all sectors.
Industrialisation, urbanisation, enhanced transportation needs, high capital expenditure
for creation of infrastructure and improvements in standard of living are contributing to the
rapid increase in energy demand.
At the 26th session of Conference of the Parties (COP 26), Glasgow, in November
2021, Hon’ble Prime Minister presented five nectar elements (Panchamrit) of India’s
03.04.2025 28
climate action which inter-alia included achieving 500GW of Non-fossil energy capacity by
2030, 50 per cent of its energy requirements from renewable energy by 2030 and
achieving the target of net zero emissions by 2070. India is the first G20 country to meet
its Paris commitments. As on 28.02.2025, a total of 222.86 GW non-fossil power capacity
has been installed against a target of 500 GW in the country which includes 102.57 GW
solar power, 48.59 GW wind power, 11.45 GW bio-power, 52.07 GW hydro power and
8.18 GW nuclear power. Further, the share of non-fossil power in the total installed
electricity capacity in the country has increased from 31.53% in 2014 to 47.37% as of
now.
India's strong commitment to clean energy is also being propelled by Ethanol
Blending Petrol Programme, Pradhan Mantri JI-VAN scheme, Compressed Bio Gas
(CBG) ecosystem, National Hydrogen Mission, etc.
As a green fuel, Ethanol supports the environmental sustainability efforts of the
Government. Government have been promoting blending of ethanol in petrol under the
Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme to reduce import dependence on crude oil
while saving foreign exchange and promote domestic agriculture sector. National Policy
on Biofuels – 2018, as amended in 2022, inter-alia, advanced the target of 20% blending
of ethanol in petrol to ESY 2025-26 from 2030. Interventions by the Government have led
to increased ethanol blending with petrol from 38 crore litres in ESY 2013-14 to 707 crore
litres in ESY 2023-24. In February 2025, ethanol blending of 19.68% has already been
achieved.
03.04.2025 29
In 2019, the Government notified the Pradhan Mantri JI-VAN (Jaiv Indhan –
Vatavaran Anukool fasal awashesh Nivaran) Yojana 2019, amended in 2024, to provide
financial support for setting up Advanced Biofuels projects in the country using
lignocellulosic biomass and other renewable feedstock. Further, towards decarbonizing of
the aviation sector, Government has approved 1% Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF)
indicative blending target in 2027 (initially for International flights).
To promote CBG, Government have initiated various schemes like Sustainable
Alternative Towards Affordable Transportation (SATAT), financial assistance for
procurement of Biomass Aggregation Machinery (BAM) and scheme for the development
of pipeline infrastructure (DPI) for transportation of CBG into the City Gas Distribution
(CGD) network. With these initiatives, 94 CBG plants, which include a major share by
private entrepreneurs, with production capacity of about 650 TPD (tonnes per day) have
already been commissioned and another 75 CBG plants with production capacity of about
550 TPD are in different stages of construction.
The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is implementing the National Green
Hydrogen Mission, with an objective to make India a global hub of production, usage, and
export of Green Hydrogen and its derivatives. Government have launched several
initiatives under the Mission, including scheme guidelines for incentive schemes for
production of Green Hydrogen and electrolyser manufacturing under the Strategic
Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme.
03.04.2025 30
(b) and (c): Government have promoted the use of LPG and Piped Natural Gas (PNG) for
clean domestic cooking fuel. To promote LPG as a clean cooking fuel, Pradhan Mantri
Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched in May 2016, to provide deposit free LPG
connection to adult women from poor households. As on 01.03.2025, there are more than
10.33 crore LPG connections released under PMUY scheme and about 33 crore LPG
active domestic customers. As a result of various initiatives, LPG coverage in the country
has improved from 62% in April 2016 to near saturation as of now. Piped Natural Gas
(PNG) is being supplied through pipeline for domestic cooking and as of January 2025,
the authorized entities have provided 1.44 crore PNG Domestic (PNG (D)) connections.
With an aim to further create environmentally sustainable cooking solutions, OMCs are
also working on innovative solutions and encouraging start ups to provide new age
solutions like Ethanol and Solar cook stoves.
(d) to (f): The Government have targeted to increase the share of natural gas in the
Primary Energy Mix from the current level of 6% to 15% by 2030. Various steps taken by
the Government in this direction include expansion of National Gas Grid Pipeline & City
Gas Distribution (CGD) network, setting up of Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Terminals,
allocation of domestic gas to Compressed Natural Gas (Transport) / Piped Natural Gas
(Domestic) CNG(T)/PNG(D) as priority sector, allowing marketing and pricing freedom
with a ceiling price to gas produced from high pressure/high temperature areas, deep
water & ultra-deep water and from coal seams etc.
03.04.2025 31
After completion of 12/12A CGD bidding round Petroleum and Natural Gas
Regulatory Board (PNGRB) has authorized entities for the development of CGD network
in 307 Geographical Areas (GAs), covering almost 100% of the mainland area for the
development of CGD network across the country. As per Minimum Work Programme
target, authorized entities have to provide approx. 12.6 crore PNG connections and
establish 18,336 CNG stations by 2034. As on January 25, the authorized entities have
established 7594 CNG stations.
With the aim to create a National Gas Grid (One Nation, One Gas Grid) and
increase the availability of natural gas across the country, Petroleum and Natural Gas
Regulatory Board (PNGRB) has authorised approximately 33,475 km natural gas pipeline
network across the country out of which 25,124 km of natural gas pipeline including spur
lines, tie-in connectivity, Sub-Transmission Pipelines (STPL) and dedicated pipelines are
already operational and a total of 10,676 km length of pipelines are under various stages
of construction. These pipelines are constructed by the various project proponents from
their own funds keeping in view the technical and commercial feasibility following the
various regulatory approvals. Further, Government have also approved (September 2016)
Viability Gap Funding of 40% Capital Grant (Rs 5,176 crore) for Jagdishpur-
Haldia/Bokaro-Dhamra Natural Gas Pipeline (JHBDPL) project and 60% (Rs 5559 crore)
VGF for North East Gas Grid (NEGG) project (January 2020), as the critical constituents
of the National Gas Grid.
(cid:174)ी (cid:174)ीरगं आ(cid:200)पा चंद ू बारणे : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, भारत म(cid:164) ऊजा(cid:170) सरु (cid:177)ा, पया(cid:170)वरण संर(cid:177)ण और कृिष
03.04.2025 32
अपिश(cid:310) (cid:255)बंधन को (cid:197)यान म(cid:164) रखते ह(cid:242)ए सीबीजी के उ(cid:194)पादन को बढ़ावा देने क(cid:236) आव(cid:212)यकता है । घरले ू इंधन
उ(cid:194)पादन से आयात िनभ(cid:170)रता घटेगी और िकसान(cid:332) को अपिश(cid:310) से आय का एक नया (cid:261)ोत िमलेगा ।
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, म(cid:167) आपके मा(cid:197)यम से माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी से जानना चाहता ह(cid:243)ं िक सीबीजी
उ(cid:194)पादन को बढ़ावा देने के िलए महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) म (cid:164) अभी तक िकतने संय(cid:253) (cid:214)थािपत िकए गए ह(cid:167) और इसके अगल े पांच
वष(cid:334) म(cid:164) देश म(cid:164) िकतने सीबीजी संय(cid:253) (cid:214)थािपत करन े क(cid:236) योजना है?
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: Hon. Speaker, Sir, the shift from traditional energy to
clean and green energy involves a number of tracks: I might mention, as we have in the
body of the answer, biofuel blending, green hydrogen, which is the energy for the future,
and as the hon. Member mentioned, compressed biogas (CBG).
Now, compressed biogas is an area where very ambitious targets have been set
by the Government. In fact, the plan is ultimately to establish a Sustainable Alternative
Towards Affordable Transportation (SATAT). The target is very large. Today, we have 94
CBG plants which are operational. Out of these 94 plants, if I might add, 11 of them are
operational in Maharashtra. We have around 70 CBG plants in process which are at
various stages of construction.
Sir, in order to encourage CBG, the Government is taking a number of steps. I will,
with your permission, list out some of the incentives or steps we are taking to popularise
the use of CBG.
One is that we have a system of assured price for the take-off of CBG through
long-term agreements with the oil marketing companies. Secondly, we have an umbrella
scheme of the National Bio-energy Programme by the MNRE, which is providing Central
03.04.2025 33
financial assistance to all kinds of CBG and biogas plants. Additional Central assistance
is also being provided for municipal solid waste-based CBG plants under the Swachh
Bharat Mission – Urban 2.0.
Amongst the other things I might mention, Sir, is that whenever somebody wants to
set up a CBG plant, the Government is providing assistance upto 50 per cent of the cost
of the plant by way of assistance. Then there is a Rs. 10 crore capital subsidy from the
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, and the inclusion of bio-manure produced from
CBG plants under the Fertilizer Control Order 1985. Market development assistance is
being provided up to Rs. 1.5 per kilogram for fertilized organic manure to promote organic
fertilizer produced from CBG projects. When you produce CBG, there is also manure
produced which is very rich and can be sold, so some assistance is provided for offtake
from there. Inclusion of CBG projects under priority sector lending by the Reserve Bank
of India has led to a Rs. 1,000 crore scheme being set up.
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) : माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी, कृपया संि(cid:177)(cid:302) जवाब दीिजए ।
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: I would be happy to share very detailed information on
this with the hon. Member.
(cid:174)ी (cid:174)ीरगं आ(cid:200)पा चंद ू बारणे : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, मं(cid:253)ी जी न े इस िवषय पर बह(cid:242)त सारी और अ(cid:184)छी
जानकारी दी है ।
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, भारत क(cid:236) ऊजा(cid:170) सरु (cid:177)ा को मजबूत करन,े पया(cid:170)वरण संर(cid:177)ण को बढ़ावा देने
और आयाितत (cid:186)वलनशील ईधं न(cid:332) पर िनभ(cid:170)रता कम करन े के िलए बायो सीएनजी, ह(cid:229)रत हाइ(cid:369)ोजन और
इथने ॉल िमि(cid:174)त पे(cid:367)ोल जैसे (cid:214)व(cid:184)छ ईधं न(cid:332) को (cid:211)यापक (cid:322)प से अपनाने क(cid:236) आव(cid:212)यकता है ।
03.04.2025 34
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, म(cid:167) माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी स े यह जानना चाहता ह(cid:243)ं िक देश म (cid:164) बायो सीएनजी, ह(cid:229)रत
हाइ(cid:369)ोजन एवं इथेनॉल िमि(cid:174)त पे(cid:367)ोल जैस े (cid:214)व(cid:184)छ ईधं न(cid:332) को बढ़ावा देने के िलए मं(cid:253)ालय (cid:300)ारा (cid:179)या ठोस कदम
उठाए गए ह(cid:167)? इसम(cid:164) िनजी िनवेश को (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन देने के िलए (cid:179)या कोई िवशेष (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन योजना को लागू करन े का
िवचार है?
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: Sir, I should like to start by providing a little explanation
that we are moving, in overall terms, from toxic fuels to cleaner fuels. This is not going to
be an easy journey; it is going to be a long journey. Even when this discussion at the
international level commenced about doing away with fossil fuels, there were people,
economists in 1976, who predicted that oil would reach a peak by the year 2000, but it
did not happen. Today, if you were to ask any economist working in the energy sector, by
when will oil peak – I am not so sure – nobody will even stick their necks out. Today,
India is consuming 6.3 per cent of the world’s primary energy, which will go up to 12.5 per
cent by the year 2050. We have to try within this process to move away from all the
traditional toxic fuels to cleaner and cleaner fuels.
The hon. Member mentioned biofuels. As far as biofuels are concerned, they are
already a clean fuel. I mean, in the year 2014, when the hon. Prime Minister assumed
responsibility, our total biofuel blending was only 1.4 per cent. In fact, in a previous era, a
target was set for five per cent biofuel blending in 10 of our States and Union Territories,
and we were never able to proceed beyond 1.4 per cent. We set ourselves a target of 10
per cent blending by November 2022, and we were able to do that five months in
advance. We set ourselves a target of 20 per cent blending six years later from now. We
03.04.2025 35
have almost reached 20 per cent. In fact, the last month’s figures that we have, as my
senior colleague would know, show that we have already reached 19.6 per cent. So,
biofuels are doing very well.
Now, you also mentioned green hydrogen. Green hydrogen, I maintain is actually
the energy, the fuel of the future. Why do I say it? It is because in order to produce green
hydrogen, all you require is clean natural green solar energy, an electrolyser, and the
ability to scale that up with new technologies like pump storage, etc.
It is proceeding very well. But the fact of the matter is that green hydrogen is a very
new development. Now, what are the targets that we are setting for ourselves? The
Government has set a target of 5,000 million tonnes for 2030. I personally believe that
with the involvement of the private sector --and the hon. Member had read that, it is too
conservative a target -- I believe with new technologies like pump storage, with the cost
of batteries internationally coming down, and batteries having more storage, green
hydrogen will take off. But when will it take off, I still think that we are only in 2025.
The fact of the matter is that we spend annually $150 billion on the import of
energy. Now, if we are in a position to produce green hydrogen and the cost is the most
important thing, the most optimistic figures I have seen is the cost of green hydrogen per
kilo would be about $5 or so, and if we can bring it down to two $2½ dollars, I can
understand that we will be in a situation where people will say, let us move from
traditional toxic fuels to green hydrogen straightaway.
I should have mentioned that our target is five million tonnes by 2030, not 5,000
03.04.2025 36
million tonnes. That was slip of the tongue. But I also want to say that the Government is
doing whatever it can. The Government introduced a PLI scheme for electrolyzers and I
think it is about Rs. 19,700 crore or Rs. 20,000 crore, and there is a very good off-take on
that.
In so far as the other fuels are concerned, let me tell you that all the green energy
has positive outputs. When we did biofuel blending, today we have saved something like
Rs. 110,000 crore on import bill and जैसा म(cid:167)ने पहले कई बार कहा है िक हमार े जो अ(cid:198)नदाता ह(cid:167), they
are also financing the Annadatas. That money has been transferred to the agricultural
sector as a result of the savings in that. So, the two are complimentary.
In regard to all the other areas of movement, there is definite progress in all the
areas, but as I would like to say, the progress has to be realistically assessed. Why do I
say that? It is because still a very large percentage of our economic needs on energy are
met from coal and from fossil fuels. We are trying to progressively move away from that
and most of our assessments all over the world said India's growth will take place. The
World bp Outlook said 4 to 6 per cent growth will take place. … (Interruptions)
I am always ready to oblige. I will just conclude by saying that the International
Energy Agency in Paris predicts that of all the growth that will take place in the next 20
years, two decades, 25 per cent of that growth in energy will come from India. It is our
collective resolve that we will make more and more and we will shift to green energy, as
is the thrust of this Question.
(cid:174)ीमती भारती पारधी : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, म(cid:167) अपने देश के यश(cid:214)वी (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी आदरणीय नर(cid:198)े (cid:254) मोदी जी को
03.04.2025 37
ध(cid:198)यवाद देना चाह(cid:243)ंगी, िजनक(cid:236) इस मह(cid:194)वाकां(cid:177)ी योजना का ि(cid:248)या(cid:198)वयन सचु ा(cid:322) (cid:322)प से पूर े देश म(cid:164) हो रहा है । म (cid:167)
आदणीय मं(cid:253)ी जी को भी ध(cid:198)यवाद देना चाहती ह(cid:243)ं, िज(cid:198)ह(cid:332)न े मुझे िव(cid:214)तृत जानकारी दी है ।
म(cid:167) जानना चाहती ह(cid:243)ं िक (cid:179)या म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) गैस ि(cid:250)ड और संबंिधत बुिनयादी ढांचे के िवकास के िलए क(cid:164) (cid:254)
सरकार (cid:300)ारा कोई िवशेष योजना बनाई गई है, और इस योजना के िलए अब तक आवंिटत धनरािश और
अनमु ािनत (cid:211)यय का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है? इसके अलावा, इस योजना के तहत गैस ि(cid:250)ड क(cid:236) उपल(cid:202)धता और िवकास
से संबंिधत अ(cid:198)य बुिनयादी ढांचे को मजबूत करन े के िलए सरकार (cid:300)ारा (cid:179)या सधु ारा(cid:194)मक कदम उठाए जा रहे
ह(cid:167)? िवशेष (cid:322)प से बालाघाट-िसवनी संसदीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164), जहां ऊजा(cid:170) और गैस आधा(cid:229)रत ढांचे क(cid:236) उपल(cid:202)धता क(cid:236)
िदशा म(cid:164) खास चुनौती है, वहां इस योजना के तहत सरकार (cid:300)ारा कौन से िविश(cid:310) (cid:255)यास िकए जा रहे ह,(cid:167) तािक
इस (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) भी िवकास काय(cid:334) को गित दी जा सके और (cid:214)थानीय जनसं(cid:180)या को इसके लाभ िमल सक(cid:164) ?
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: Sir, after the 12th and 12A CGD bidding round, the
Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board has authorized entities for the development
of CGD network in 307 geographical areas, which almost covers 100 per cent of our
mainland territory.
In fact, we are now moving to the island territories and other areas. But typically,
what happens when you set up a plant wherever the CBG is being produced is that you
need to provide connectivity to the existing pipeline. So, the Government is also providing
incentives to make sure that this is going to move forward and take it to the pipeline so
that the CBG producer is able to get a return on that straightway.
There are other incentives which I had mentioned when I was responding to the
hon. Member earlier. Insofar as the areas which were covered, you have sought the
figures for Madhya Pradesh. I will share the figures straightway. I will just add the figures
03.04.2025 38
regarding Maharashtra with me. There are plants by HPCL, MNGL, and IOCL in different
places. But in all the States of the country, we are doing that. I will provide the precise
figures for Madhya Pradesh in a minute. We have installed 17 CBG plants in Madhya
Pradesh. I also have the figures for districts. And wherever a CBG plant comes up, the
economic benefits also come along for the local people. It also benefits the agricultural
community and others. I am very happy to sit with the hon. Member and go through our
experience in respect of the 94 CBG plants which are already operational, and we are
also thinking what more we can do in order to further generate economic activity through
this.
SHRIMATI PRATIMA MONDAL: Sir, I would like to know from the hon. Minister, through
you, Sir, what steps is the Ministry taking to attract domestic and foreign investment in
clean energy initiatives. Are there any research and development initiatives focusing on
carbon capture, storage, and utilization in collaboration with global partners?
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177): माननीय सद(cid:214)य, कृपया स(cid:177)ं ेप म(cid:164) (cid:255)(cid:307) पूछ(cid:164) और सं(cid:177)ेप म (cid:164) उ(cid:176)र िदया जाए ।
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: Sir, the specific question is, whether we have a system
whereby we seek or allow foreign direct investment or foreign collaboration. Yes, a green
hydrogen, I think, is a case in point. I think one of the entities which is operating in that
sector, if memory serves me right, has something like 32 billion dollars of foreign
investment or collaborations which have come in for the production of green hydrogen. In
the first instance, they are producing green ammonia which is being sold to entities in
Europe and Singapore. There is an entity in Singapore; they are setting up a new
03.04.2025 39
generation electricity plant with GE, and the green ammonia is going to be supplied by an
Indian party. So, the answer is yes.
Insofar as our overall policies are concerned, it is our intention to take natural gas
as a percentage in the energy mix from the current six per cent to 15 per cent. I would
have been much happier if I could say that we are close to 15 per cent. No! I think we are
still closer to six per cent, but we are moving clearly, and in the coming few years, we will
be inching forward to 15 per cent. This involves a foreign investment of 72 billion dollars
that will come from foreign investment. The technologies are incubated here. I can tell
you for instance, in the case of biofuels, some of the second-generation plants which
have been set up are all domestically produced technologies. I can tell you about a 2G
plant in Panipat operated by IOCL. It is a local party of Pune which has developed the
technology. The bamboo plant is also there.
Insofar as carbon capture is concerned, I have met various global CEOs of
companies like Occidental and Schlumberger which use carbon sequestration for
enhanced oil recovery. But again, this is a larger subject. I would be very happy to sit with
the hon. Member. I am always ready for a discussion like this.
डॉ. िनिशका(cid:198)त दुबे : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) जी, मेरा बड़ा ही िस(cid:204)पल सा सवाल है । (cid:255)धान मं(cid:253)ी जी न े (cid:181)लासगो म(cid:164) पंचामतृ का

से जोड़ना चाहते ह(cid:167), िजसके बार े म(cid:164) उ(cid:198)ह(cid:332)ने कहा था । उसके िलए पूर े देश म(cid:164) 309 िबिडंग क(cid:236) गई है, तािक िसटी

03.04.2025 40
टे(cid:179)नोलॉजी है, (cid:179)य(cid:332)िक एक समय पर कोयला ख(cid:194)म हो जाएगा । इसिलए (cid:179)या भारत सरकार कोयला मं(cid:253)ालय के
साथ िमलकर कोल बेड मीथेन का ए(cid:179)स(cid:200)लोरशे न करके इ(cid:204)पोट(cid:170) िबल को घटाएगी तथा (cid:179)या उसके मा(cid:197)यम स े
िसटी गैस िड(cid:214)(cid:367)ी(cid:202)यूट करेगी?
SHRI HARDEEP SINGH PURI: Sir, I don't think I am the correct person to answer when
coal will be exhausted or when we will move to biogas and other cleaner energies. I can
tell you that the effort on behalf of all my senior colleagues who are present here and
otherwise is to move more and more rapidly in all forms of energy utilisation, whether it is
transportation or refineries, to move to green energy.
The specific question on when we will be able to move away from coal is not easy
to answer. When the discussion on energy transition took place internationally, very
ambitious targets were set for energy transition. But I think some very basic mistakes
were also made. You cannot transition from the present to the future unless you have the
green fuel ready to transition away from coal. You cannot say you will stop using coal and
fossil fuel like crude oil and suddenly not have a replacement. So today, the effort is to
redouble on that. Insofar as this is concerned, I have recently taken a review of ONGC
and they have recently started coal-bed methane in several areas.
Again, that is a subject which we need to discuss and see what more can be done.
But I can assure you that even the oil marketing companies which have traditionally dealt
with fossil fuels and crude oil, are also increasingly having their attention occupied by the
transition to green energy.

SHRI B. MANICKAM TAGORE: Hon. Speaker Sir, in the statement, the hon. Minister
states that 9,21,448 trainees were trained at the operational IDTRs from 2015 to 2023,
with a peak of 1,59,182 in 2019-20, followed by a decline in COVID times. However, no
direct correlation is provided between this training and the reduction in the road accidents
caused by driver errors, given that India reported 4,61,312 road accidents in 2022, with
over 70 per cent attributed to human error as per the Ministry's data. Can the hon.
03.04.2025 47
Minister clarify how the Government assesses the scheme's impact on reducing such
accidents, and what specific metrics or studies link the training of 9,21,448 drivers to
improve road safety outcomes?
SHRI NITIN JAIRAM GADKARI: Hon. Speaker Sir, one of the important things related
with road safety is the training of drivers. Actually, in India, we have a shortage of 22 lakh
skilled drivers. It is from the World Bank report. Firstly, it is very important that we need to
have good facilities for training drivers. Not only that, we are now going to start learner's
license module with Aadhaar-based face authentication. On that basis, a person can use
his mobile or his computer, he can give that test, and after passing that test, he will get a
certificate or license for driving.
The test can be given from home also. We have three schemes. One is the
Institute of Driving Training and Research (IDTR). The second is the Regional Driving
Training Centre (RDTC), and the third is the District Driving Training Centre (DTC). So, it
is very important. Our Ministry has taken a new decision and has now launched a policy
regarding Driving Training Centres, which was launched on 7th January, 2025. The
capital investment for this scheme is Rs.4,500 crore. This scheme is going to generate
50 lakh employments. So, 1,600 institutes will be set up in the three-tier system with an
additional grant of Rs.1.5 crore for every District Training Centre with fitness station
cluster. Recently, I have sanctioned 12 proposals worth Rs.33.5 crore for RDTCs and
DTCs under the new policy in the States of Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya
Pradesh and Maharashtra, out of which eight proposals are in the rural and aspirational
03.04.2025 48
districts. So, my request to all the Members of Parliament is this. This is a very
important scheme. The Government is giving a support of Rs.2.5 crore per Driving
Training Centre, particularly in the area which is socially, economically and educationally
backward, the tribal area, aspirational districts and aspirational tahsils. If we can start a
Driving Training Centre, it can be an opportunity for them to get employment.
Under this new policy, already 32 new proposals are received. Already, we have
called the meeting of Transport Ministers. We have a full discussion on this issue. This
new policy is very friendly and supportive, and it is giving more grants. That is to be a
very attractive scheme. So, there will be one DTC in each district, with priority to rural
and aspirational districts. In the North Eastern States, and also in the Union Territories,
one RDTC will be set up irrespective of population.
This is a three-tier system. The first tier is the institute at the State-level. Already,
22 drivers is the capacity of this centre. Financial support from the Government is
Rs.17.25 crore. The population criteria is 2.5 crore. Under the new policy, 30 centres
are to be sanctioned. In tier-two, that is Regional Driving Training Centres (RDTCs), the
annual training capacity is 11,000 drivers, financial support is Rs.5.5 crore, and
population criteria is one crore. Previously, in the old policy, it was 2.5 crore. Under the
new policy, 125 centres are to be sanctioned. In tier-three, that is District Training
Centre, the annual training capacity is 6,700 drivers; financial support is Rs.2.5 crore. In
the old one, it was Rs.1 crore. Population criteria is 10 lakhs. In the old one, it was 20
lakhs. Under the new policy, we can sanction 1,500 Driver Training Schools. I request all
03.04.2025 49
the Members of Parliament and all the State Governments that if they can take
advantage of this scheme, this scheme is going to create more employment potential in
the backward areas.
One of the reasons is related to accidents. This is also very important. Already, in
our country, we have 1,80,000 deaths. There are 30,000 deaths because of the truck
drivers, without licence. So, this is the time that in our country, we have to make driving
training of international standard. It is very important. India is the country in the whole
world where you can get a driving licence very easily. We have to make it with a correct
procedure to make it qualitatively, and we are trying for that.
SHRI B. MANICKAM TAGORE: Sir, the hon. Minister has given a detailed reply. I
represent an aspirational district called Virudhunagar in Tamil Nadu. In his statement,
the hon. Minister indicated that out of 87 sanctioned centres, only 27 are operational now
with just three out of the 41 DTCs functional. This leaves over 90 per cent of the
sanctioned DTCs non-operational.
What are the primary reasons for the significant delay in operationalising these
centres, particularly the DTCs meant for rural and aspirational districts, including the one
which is being sanctioned at Virudhunagar district? The District Administration has
handed over the land but there is no progress in this direction.
What are the concrete timelines the Government has set to ensure that all the 87
centres are fully operational by the end of the 15th Finance Commission cycle in 2025-
26? Thank you, Sir.
03.04.2025 50
SHRI NITIN JAIRAM GADKARI: Hon. Speaker, Sir, actually, we had only received two
proposals from the State Government of Tamil Nadu previously. But after declaring the
new policy, we have not received any proposal from Tamil Nadu.
The IDTR-III centres are required in Tamil Nadu; an RTDC-7 is required in Tamil
Nadu; a DTC-78 is required in Tamil Nadu. I am always trying to convince the Transport
Minister and the State Government to send a proposal. And for that reason, we have now
relaxed that condition that your proposal should be recommended by the RTO or by the
Transport Ministry. Now, we have given an alternative that even a collector can
recommend it and we can consider that.
So, my suggestion is this. I am open for that. We are ready to sanction it. We only
need an application from the State of Tamil Nadu. There is a huge potential which is
available. Particularly for Tirunelveli, CRT observation is shared with the State and
response is awaited. With respect to Salem, CRT observation is shared with the State
and response is awaited.
My suggestion to the hon. Member is, please convince your Transport Minister and
please send me the proposal. I am ready to sanction it.
SHRI VIJAYAKUMAR ALIAS VIJAY VASANTH : Thank you, hon. Speaker, Sir.
The revised guidelines in January, 2025 have enhanced Central assistance for
DCCs. However, given the persistent shortage of trained commercial vehicle drivers, how
does the Government plan to address the increase in demand for trained commercial
vehicle drivers?
03.04.2025 51
SHRI NITIN JAIRAM GADKARI: Sir, actually, the World Bank Report indicates that we
have a shortage of 22 lakh drivers. There is an increase in economic growth. And the
problem is that with an increase in economic growth, 80 per cent of our goods traffic is on
the road. Now, our drivers are working for 16 hours. As per the labour laws, it is not
correct and it is not lawful. But the problem is that if you are going to restrict it to eight
hours, there will be a big shortage of drivers. So, today, it is important that we have to
train the drivers. There is a huge potential for employment. The Government is very
positive about it.
Our new policy, which has already been declared in January, is very friendly. Even
I am requesting all the Members of Parliament to start these Driving Training Centres in
their constituencies, which are going to create employment potential. Even you can start
a fitness centre there. You can start a scrapping centre. Making this cluster is
economically viable. The Government is giving special grants for all these things. So, it is
to be professionally and economically viable. And that is the reason, today, our country
has a huge potential in the field of drivers. There is a huge potential for scrapping. There
is a huge potential for fitness centres. Already, the Government has formulated a law for
that.
In this situation, these proposals are very attractive and now the policy is very
friendly. I am requesting the Members of Parliament and all the State Governments that -
- under the leadership of Prime Minister Modi ji -- on the basis of the friendly policy that
we have, you can think about it and take the initiative. My Ministry is ready to cooperate
03.04.2025 52
and support you.

03.04.2025 55
(घ) सरकार ने इस त(cid:195)य के (cid:320)ि(cid:310)गत िक लोग मुरादाबाद म(cid:164) पीतल और ह(cid:214)तिश(cid:208)प (cid:211)यवसाय के िलए आते ह(cid:167),
मुरादाबाद को िद(cid:208)ली, मुंबई, कोलकाता, च(cid:198)े नई और अ(cid:198)य महानगर(cid:332) से िवमान सेवाओ ंस े जोड़ने के िलए (cid:179)या
कदम उठाए ह(cid:167)?
THE MINISTER OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI KINJARAPU RAMMOHAN NAIDU):
(a) to (d): A Statement is laid on the Table of the House.
STATEMENT
(a): Flight operations on Moradabad-Lucknow route were commenced by Flybig Airline on
10.08.2024 under the RCS-UDAN scheme. The airline, however, discontinued the flight
operation due to poor visibility in North India from 03.11.2024. At present, resurfacing
work is in progress at Lucknow airport in a phased manner and the runway is closed
during the day time, hence the flight operations cannot be resumed since Moradabad is a
VFR airport.;
(b) and (c): The Government launched Regional Connectivity Scheme (RCS)-UDAN
scheme on 21-10-2016 to facilitate / stimulate regional air connectivity by reviving
unserved and underserved airports in Tier-2 & Tier3 cities. 625 RCS routes connecting 88
unserved and underserved airports including 13 heliports and 02 water aerodromes, have
been operationalised across the country under the Scheme. More than 149 lakhs
domestic passengers have travelled through RCS-UDAN in more than 2.97 lakh RCS
flights.; The Government has announced its plan to launch modified UDAN Scheme to
enhance regional connectivity to 120 new destinations and carry 4 crore passengers in
the next 10 years. The scheme will also support helipads and smaller airports in hilly,
03.04.2025 56
aspirational, and North East region districts.; The Government does not generate any
revenue by operationalising UDAN routes.
(d): UDAN is a demand-driven scheme wherein airline operators assess the feasibility of
operations on a particular route and bid under the scheme from time to time. If any airline
submits a valid bid for the operation of RCS flights connecting Moradabad to Delhi,
Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai and other metro cities in future bidding rounds, the same will
be considered as per provisions of UDAN Scheme.
Further, with the repeal of Air Corporation Act in March 1994, the Indian domestic aviation
has been deregulated. Airlines are free to induct capacity with any aircraft type, free to
select whatever markets and network/route they wish to service, depending on their
commercial and operational feasibility.
SHRI JAGADISH SHETTAR: Thank you Sir for giving me an opportunity to supplement
a question.
Here, the important thing is that the Union Government launched the Regional
Connectivity Scheme (UDAN Scheme) on 21.10.2016 to facilitate and stimulate regional
air connectivity by having unserved and underserved airports in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
It is because of the UDAN Scheme airports have developed in many Tier 2 cities.
Take for example, Belgaum. Due to the UDAN Scheme, Belgaum has gained
connectivity to more than 14 cities. However, my only request and insistence to the Civil
Aviation Minister is that after the lapse of the UDAN Scheme, all airlines are withdrawing
their flights to other cities.
03.04.2025 57
We initially had 14 cities connected, but now we have only seven or eight cities. Is
there any consideration for the extension of the Scheme for Tier 2 cities? If this Scheme
is extended, it will lead to the development of the airports.
SHRI KINJARAPU RAMMOHAN NAIDU: Sir, the hon. Member has asked a very
important question that often comes into discussion: how long will the UDAN Scheme
run? When the bidding for the UDAN scheme is completed and the airlines are ready to
operate the route, it is granted for three years. The Government’s intention is not to
continuously fund the airlines or passenger airfare, but to provide a kick-start to
operations from that airport, especially on routes where viability is lacking.
That is why, we have stated that UDAN Scheme will serve unserved areas, where
there are no operations at all, or underserved areas, where there are fewer than seven
flights operating from that airport. For instance, at Belgaum Airport, we initiated the
UDAN scheme, and now, if you observe, there are more than 98 flights operating from
Bangalore, Delhi, Shamshabad, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Jaipur, Nagpur, and Tirupati. Due
to the demand, many operations are now in place.
However, even after three years of running that route, if we still find that
connectivity to those airports is inadequate, we can bid for the route again within a one-
year cooling period, allowing the UDAN Scheme to restart itself for another three years.
Moreover, the UDAN Scheme was originally envisaged for ten years. It started in 2016
and is up to 2026. We anticipated that this would be the period during which regional
connectivity would receive a significant boost.
03.04.2025 58
Under the visionary leadership of our Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi ji, we
have now extended it for another ten years. Not only that, but we also aspire to connect
120 new destinations. So, this is just the beginning. We believe there is substantial scope
for the growth of aviation in the country. When it comes to the UDAN Scheme, let us all
remember that we are implementing it to kick start the operations, not to continuously
fund the route itself.

बात क(cid:236) जाएगी । इसके साथ ही, आजमगढ़ एयरपोट(cid:170), कुशीनगर एयरपोट(cid:170) भी है, ऐसे चार-पाचँ एयरपोट्(cid:170)स ह(cid:167) ।
उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश एक ऐसा रा(cid:186)य है, जहा ँ आज 16 एयरपोट्(cid:170)स ह(cid:167) । उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) शायद सबसे (cid:186)यादा एयरपोट्(cid:170)स ह(cid:167) ।
यह (cid:214)वाभािवक है िक िजतने भी एयरपोट्(cid:170)स ह(cid:167), सबस े पहले उनक(cid:236) कनेि(cid:179)टिवटी राजधानी से हो, यही उनक(cid:236)
इ(cid:184)छा रहती है । िजतनी एयरलाइंस न े भी जहा ँ के िलए िबड िकया है, जहा ँ पर भी अवॉड(cid:165)ड है, हम उनस े बात
करग(cid:164) े िक वे ज(cid:208)दी से ज(cid:208)दी कनेि(cid:179)टिवटी श(cid:322)ु कर (cid:164) ।

WRITTEN ANSWERS TO QUESTIONS
(i)Starred Question Nos. 467 to 480
*467. SHRI SAPTAGIRI SANKAR ULAKA:
SHRI CHARANJIT SINGH CHANNI:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
03.04.2025 64
(a) the total number of rural households in the Koraput and Jalandhar Parliamentary
Constituencies as per the latest available data;
(b) the number of rural households provided with Functional Household Tap Connections
(FHTCs) under the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – 'Har Ghar Jal' scheme in the said
Constituencies since its inception in August 2019, year-wise;
(c) the number of rural households yet to receive FHTCs under the said scheme in the
said Constituencies; and
(d) the total funds allocated, disbursed and utilized for the implementation of the said
scheme in the said Constituencies since August 2019, year-wise?
THE MINISTER OF JAL SHAKTI (SHRI C. R. PATIL):
(a) to (c): Since August 2019, Government of India is implementing Jal Jeevan Mission
(JJM), in partnership with States/ UTs, to make provision of tap water supply to every
rural household of the country. Drinking Water is a state subject, and hence, the
responsibility of planning, approval, implementation, operation, and maintenance of
drinking water supply schemes, including those under JJM, lies with State/UT
Governments. The Government of India supports the states by providing technical and
financial assistance.
As reported by state of Odisha, there are 5,32,290 rural households in Koraput
parliamentary constituency and tap water connections have been provided 3,96,592 rural
households since the launch of JJM in Koraput parliamentary constituency leaving a
03.04.2025 65
balance of 1,28,649 rural households which are yet to be covered. The year-wise
coverage in Koraput parliamentary constituency has been given below:
Year No. of FHTCs provided during the year
2019-20 18,080
2020-21 68,458
2021-22 73,018
2022-23 84,032
2023-24 1,06,579
2024-25 46,425
Total 3,96,592
As reported by state of Punjab, all the 2,24,197 rural households in Jalandhar
parliamentary constituency have been provided with tap water connections. Out of which,
99,542 rural households that were provided tap water connection after launch of Jal
Jeevan Mission. The year-wise coverage in Jalandhar constituency has been given
below:
Year No. of FHTCs provided During the year
2019-20 3,329
2020-21 61,465
2021-22 33,805
2022-23 -
2023-24 -
2024-25 943
Total 99,542
(d):Under JJM, funds are released directly to the States/ UTs. The parliamentary
constituency/ district-wise fund details are not maintained at the Government of India
03.04.2025 66
level. The details of fund allocation, fund drawn and reported fund utilization during the
last five years (2019-20, 2020-21, 2021-22, 2022-23 and 2023-24) and current financial
year 2024-25 (as on 31.03.2025) by the state of Odisha are as under:
(Amount in Rs. Crore)
Central

Opening Available Reported
Allocation Fund Drawn
share
Balance Fund utilization

सहायता (सीए) है। 2011 क(cid:236) जनगणना के अनुसार 3-40 लाख क(cid:236) आबादी वाले शहर और रा(cid:186) य(cid:332) क(cid:236) 3 लाख
से कम आबादी वाली अ(cid:198) य राजधािनया ँ इस योजना के तहत सहायता के िलए पा(cid:253) ह(cid:167)। इस योजना के तहत
िबहार रा(cid:186)य के 6 शहर(cid:332) म (cid:164) 400 बस(cid:164) (cid:214)वीकृत क(cid:236) गई ह(cid:167)। कुल संिवत(cid:229)रत रािश 87.55 करोड़ (cid:321)पये है।
PROGRESS OF PIPED WATER AND SEWERAGE CONNECTIONS
UNDER AMRUT
*469. SHRI VISHALDADA PRAKASHBAPU PATIL:
03.04.2025 71
Will the Minister of Housing and Urban Affairs be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has formulated any strategy to expand urban household
access to piped water and sewerage systems under Atal Mission for Rejuvenation
and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) and other related schemes;
(b) if so, the details of the key infrastructure projects along with their expected impact
on improving urban sanitation and water supply;
(c) whether the Government has any data on the progress of the piped water and
sewerage connections achieved under AMRUT;
(d) if so, the details thereof, State-wise along with the current coverage levels and
targets set for the next phase of implementation; and
(e) whether any technological advancements or smart water management solutions
are being integrated to enhance efficiency and sustainability in urban water supply
systems and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS; AND MINISTER OF POWER
(SHRI MANOHAR LAL):
(a) and (b): Water and Sanitation are State subjects and their management is the
responsibility of the State Government. Government of India supplements the efforts of
the States through schematic interventions/ advisories. It provides financial and technical
support to the States through various schemes/ Missions such as Atal Mission for
Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) and AMRUT 2.0.
03.04.2025 72
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) is implementing AMRUT, which
was launched on 25 June 2015 in selected 500 cities and towns (now 485 cities including
15 merged cities) across the country. MoHUA has approved State Annual Action Plans
(SAAPs) of all the States/Union Territories (UTs) amounting to ₹77,640 crore for the entire
Mission period, which includes committed Central Assistance (CA) of ₹35,990 crore. So
far, States/UTs have grounded 6,010 projects worth ₹83,578 crore including 2,295
projects worth ₹77,935 crore in water/ sewerage sector. Overall, works worth around
₹79,401 crore have been physically completed and expenditure of ₹72,656 crore has
been incurred.
Under AMRUT Mission and in convergence, so far 189 lakh water tap connections
(new/ serviced) provided against target of 139 lakh; 149 lakh sewer connections (new/
serviced) (including households covered through Faecal Sludge and Septage
Management-FSSM) provided against target of 145 lakh; Water supply network of 64,463
kilometres & sewer network of 19,598 kilometres constructed; 4,734 Million Litre Per Day
(MLD) Water Treatment Capacity (WTP) and 4,447 Million Litre Per Day (MLD) Sewerage
Treatment Capacity (STP) developed of which 1,437 MLD STP developed for
recycle/reuse; 1,434 km length of drains constructed with elimination of 3,708 numbers of
water logging points; 5,086 acres of green space developed.
Further to expand the coverage in urban household access to piped water and
sewerage systems, AMRUT 2.0 has been launched on 01 October 2021 in all Urban
Local Bodies (ULBs)/ cities, enabling the cities to become 'self-reliant' and 'water secure'.
03.04.2025 73
Providing universal coverage of sewerage & septage management in 500 AMRUT cities
is one of the major focus areas of AMRUT 2.0.
Under AMRUT and AMRUT 2.0, States/ Union Territories (UTs) are empowered to
select, prioritize, design, implement and monitor the projects within the broad framework
of Mission guidelines. So far, under AMRUT 2.0 a total of 3,568 water supply projects
worth ₹1,14,220.62 crore and 592 sewerage and septage management projects worth
₹67,607.67 crore (including Operations & Maintenance cost) have been approved by
MoHUA.
State-wise details of water and sewerage connections and tap coverage as reported
by the States is given in Statement-I.
(c) and (d): As per the information provided by the States in 2023, the tap coverage has
increased to 70% from 49% in 2011 through the joint efforts of States and
AMRUT/AMRUT 2.0. Further, 181 lakh new tap connections, 67.11 lakh new sewer
connections, 10,647 MLD water treatment plant capacity and 6,739 MLD sewage
treatment plant capacity have been approved under AMRUT 2.0 projects. State-wise
targets in approved water and sewerage projects are given in Statement-II.
(e): Smart elements, components and technologies are part of AMRUT projects and
this aims to promote sustainable urban development. AMRUT guidelines provides for
smart elements such as Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) as part of the
water supply and sewerage projects. As reported by States/ Union Territories (UTs), 230
03.04.2025 74
Water supply projects and 146 Sewerage projects have been implemented with SCADA.
Further, as reported by States/UTs under AMRUT 2.0, 1,487 water supply projects, 235
Sewerage projects are approved with SCADA.
Technology Sub-Mission is another important component of AMRUT 2.0 to
encourage start-up ideas and private entrepreneurship, and commissioning them into the
pilot projects. Under this Sub-Mission, 120 Start-ups have been shortlisted so far and
mapped with 82 AMRUT cities.

(cid:214)वीकृित के िलए अि(cid:250)म (cid:322)प से रा(cid:186)य के िह(cid:214) से का (cid:255)ावधान करना होता है और समत(cid:208)ु य रा(cid:186)य के िह(cid:214) से के
साथ वा(cid:214)तव म(cid:164) िनिधयां जारी क(cid:236) जाती ह(cid:167)।
03.04.2025 81
िद(cid:208)ली म(cid:164) िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) के िलए भूिम का आवंटन
*
471. (cid:174)ी रामवीर िसहं िबधूड़ीः
(cid:179)या आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) मं(cid:253)ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िकः
(क) (cid:179)या िवगत दस वष(cid:334) के दौरान िद(cid:208)ली सरकार ने िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना के िलए िद(cid:208)ली िवकास (cid:255)ािधकरण
(डीडीए) से भूिम का आवंटन करन े के िलए कहा है और यिद हां, तो िवगत दस वष(cid:334) के दौरान (cid:255)ा(cid:302) ऐसे (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव(cid:332)
क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या और (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ख) िद(cid:208)ली सरकार को उ(cid:265) अविध के दौरान वष(cid:170)वार िकतने िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) के िलए भूिम आवंिटत क(cid:236) गई है और
उनका (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है; और
(ग) ऐसे िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) का (cid:202)यौरा और सं(cid:180)या िकतनी है िजनके िलए िद(cid:208)ली सरकार ने सभी औपचा(cid:229)रकताए ं पूरी
करने के बाद भूिम पर क(cid:202)जा ले िलया है?
आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा िव(cid:299)ुत म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी मनोहर लाल):
(क): जी, हा।ँ िद(cid:208)ली सरकार न े िवगत दस वष(cid:334) के दौरान िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:214) थापना के िलए िद(cid:208)ली िवकास
(cid:255)ािधकरण (डीडीए) को 25 (cid:200)लॉट/भूिम हेतु अनुरोध (cid:255)(cid:214)तुत िकए ह(cid:167)। (cid:202)यौरा िववरण-I म(cid:164) है।
(ख) और (ग): िद(cid:208)ली सरकार को िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:214) थापना के िलए आवंिटत भूिम तथा उस पर क(cid:202)जे क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित
का (cid:202)यौरा िववरण-II म(cid:164) िदया गया है।

चली गई है। चूंिक ये (cid:177)े(cid:253) ल(cid:167)ड पूिलंग जोन के अंतग(cid:170)त आते ह(cid:167), इसिलए िवषयगत भूिम को स(cid:335)पा नह(cid:233) गया है और ल(cid:167)ड पूिलंग

ह(cid:242)ई थी। इसे वष(cid:170) 2019 म(cid:164) एनसीएलटी म(cid:164) एनएचपीसी क(cid:236) बोली के मा(cid:197)यम से िफर से शु(cid:322) िकया
गया है।
(iii) िसि(cid:179)कम म(cid:164) रिं गत IV (120 मेगावाट) मूल (cid:322)प से जल पावर कॉप(cid:333)रेशन िलिमटेड (िनजी (cid:177)े(cid:253))
को आवंिटत क(cid:236) गई थी और अ(cid:179)टूबर, 2013 से (cid:321)क(cid:236) ह(cid:242)ई थी। एनसीएलटी म(cid:164) एनएचपीसी क(cid:236)
बोली के मा(cid:197)यम से प(cid:229)रयोजना को िफर से शु(cid:322) िकया गया है और एनएचपीसी ने िदनांक
31.03.2021 को जल पावर कॉप(cid:333)रेशन िलिमटेड का अिध(cid:250)हण कर िलया है।
(iv) ज(cid:204)मू-क(cid:212)मीर म (cid:164) रतल े एचईपी (850 मेगावाट) मूल (cid:322)प से जीवीके को आवंिटत क(cid:236) गई थी और
वष(cid:170) 2014 से (cid:321)क(cid:236) ह(cid:242)ई थी। एनएचपीसी, जेके एसपीडीसी और पीडीडी, ज(cid:204)मू-क(cid:212)मीर के बीच एक
समझौता (cid:178)ापन पर ह(cid:214)ता(cid:177)र होने के बाद इसे िफर से शु(cid:322) िकया गया।
(छ) : सरकार ने जलिव(cid:299)तु प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंको समय पर पूरा करने के िलए िन(cid:204)निलिखत (cid:255)यास िकए ह(cid:167):

म(cid:164) कमी लाने संबंधी उपाय जारी िकए गए ह(cid:167)।

क(cid:236) िनयिु (cid:265) करके (cid:255)ारिं भक चरण म(cid:164) हाई(cid:369)ो सीपीएसयू (cid:300)ारा िन(cid:213)पािदत अनुबंध(cid:332) के सिं वदा(cid:194)मक
