NATIONAL SCHEDULED TRIBES FINANCE AND DEVELOPMENT
CORPORATION
5422. SHRI PRABHAKAR REDDY VEMIREDDY:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the schemes being implemented by National Scheduled Tribes
Finance and Development Corporation (NSTFDC) for income generation and self-
employment of tribals;
(b) the extent to which Adivasi Mahila Sashaktikaran Yojana is helping tribal
women along with details of the loans given to tribal women in Andhra Pradesh
during the last five years and the current year, year and district-wise;
(c) whether it is true that the loan is provided only up to Rupees two lakhs and
there have been demands for increasing the same; and
(d) if so, the details thereof along with the steps taken/proposed to be taken by
the Government to increase the loan amount to Rupees ten lakhs?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
03.04.2025 829
(a): National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation (NSTFDC),
a Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE) under the Ministry of Tribal Affairs,
provides credit linkage by extending concessional loans to the eligible Scheduled
Tribe persons through State Channelising Agencies (SCAs) for undertaking
income generation activities/ self-employment under its various schemes. The
detail of schemes under which the loans are being extended is given below:
Term Loan Scheme: NSTFDC provides Term Loan for viable projects costing
upto ₹50 lakh per unit. Under the scheme, financial assistance is extended upto
90% of the cost of the project and the balance is met by way of subsidy/ promoter
contribution/ margin money.
Adivasi Mahila Sashaktikaran Yojana (AMSY): This is an exclusive
scheme for economic development of Scheduled Tribes Women. Under the
scheme, NSTFDC provides loan upto 90% for projects costing upto ₹2 lakh.
Micro Credit Scheme for Self Help Groups (MCF): This is an exclusive scheme
for Self Help Groups for meeting small loan requirement of ST member. Under the
scheme, the Corporation provides loans upto ₹50,000/- per member and
maximum ₹5 Lakh per Self Help Group (SHG).
Adivasi Shiksha Rrinn Yojana (ASRY): This is an Education loan scheme
to enable the ST students to meet expenditure for pursuing technical and
professional education including Ph.D. in India. Under this scheme, the
03.04.2025 830
Corporation provides financial assistance upto ₹10.00 lakh per eligible family. The
students are eligible for interest subsidy from Ministry of Education, Govt. of India
during the moratorium period i.e. course period plus one year after completion of
course or six months after getting the job, whichever is earlier.
(b) :Under the scheme of Adivasi Mahila Sashaktikaran Yojana (AMSY), NSTFDC
has not received any proposal from the state of Andhra Pradesh during the last
five years and current year (upto 28.03.2025). However, the Corporation has
assisted women under Micro Credit Scheme for SHG members through Stree
Nidhi Credit Co-Operative Federation, working exclusively for women SHGs. The
detail of women assisted in the State of Andhra Pradesh under Micro Credit
Scheme for SHGs during the last five years and current year up to 28.03.2025 is
given below:
S. No. Financial Year No. of Women Beneficiaries
1. 2019-20 25
2. 2020-21 12806
3. 2021-22 2004
4. 2022-23 11787
5. 2023-24 24792
6. 2024-25 (Up to 28.03.2025) 12000
03.04.2025 831
(c) to (d) :NSTFDC has not received any proposals from the implementing
agencies regarding increase in the quantum of loan limit under Adivasi Mahila
Sashaktikaran Yojana (AMSY).
EXPANSION OF DHARTI AABA MISSION IN JAMMU AND KASHMIR
5423. DR. HEMANT VISHNU SAVARA:
SHRI SHANKAR LALWANI:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
the manner in which the Union Government is collaborating with the Government
of Jammu and Kashmir, local authorities and other relevant stakeholders to
ensure the smooth implementation and success of the expanded Dharti Aaba
Mission?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan comprises of 25 interventions
implemented by 17 line Ministries and aims to saturate infrastructural gaps in
63,843 villages, improve access to health, education, Anganwadi facilities and
providing livelihood opportunities in 549 districts and 2,911 blocks in 30
States/UTs in 5 years including 393 villages in 112 blocks of 20 districts in UT of
03.04.2025 832
Jammu and Kashmir. Each concerned Ministry has been allocated budget and
targets under Abhiyan and responsible for implementing the intervention assigned
to it. The Abhiyan aims at Saturation through convergence and outreach. Ministry
of Tribal Affairs has also requested State Governments / UTs Administrations /
District Administrations including Jammu and Kashmir for organizing IEC camps
which are aimed at awareness generation on the Abhiyan and facilitating in
preparation of basic documents like Aadhar Card, Jandhan Bank account etc
which are required for availing benefits under different schemes. Further, based
on the proposals of UT of Jammu and Kashmir, Ministry of Tribal Affairs has
approved projects worth Rs.8.77 Cr under the abhiyan.
EMISSIONS FROM TRANSPORT SECTOR
5424. SHRI ADITYA YADAV :
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken cognizance of a recent study conducted
by The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI) which stated that emissions from
the country's transport sector could significantly impact climate goals, as vehicle
numbers and fuel demand are projected to soar through 2070; and
(b) if so, the details of the preventive steps proposed to be taken by the
Government keeping in view the fact that the country's transport sector is driving
03.04.2025 833
a surge in fuel consumption and carbon emissions, with potential global
implications
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) and (b): 1. Government in the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways has
taken the following key initiatives :
(i) Notified Bharat Stage (BS) VI Emission limits including use of alternative fuels
vide G.S.R 889 dated 16.09.2016 to reduce pollutants from motor vehicles.
(ii) Notified E20 as a Mono fuel vide G.S.R 27 (E), dated 5th January, 2024 for all
category of vehicles.
(iii) Notified Hydrogen as a fuel vide GSR 885(E) dated 16th December 2022 for
ICE BS-IV vehicles.
(iv) Notified the Vehicle Scrapping Policy vide G.S.R 720 dated 5th October 2021
to phase out older, unfit polluting vehicles under the framework of the Motor
Vehicles Act, 1988 and Central Motor Vehicle Rules, 1989.
2. Government has taken following initiatives to promote adoption of Electric
Vehicles (EVs) in the country:
03.04.2025 834
(i) Notification issued vide S.O. 5333(E) dated the 18th October, 2018 to grant
exemption from the requirements of the permit to the battery operated transport
vehicles.
(ii) Notification issued vide G.S.R. 525(E) dated the 2nd August, 2021 to grant
exemption of Battery Operated Vehicles from payment of fees for the purpose of
issue or renewal of registration certificate and assignment of new registration
mark.
(iii) Notification issued vide G.S.R. 302(E) dated the 18th April, 2023 to grant
exemption of All India Tourist Permit for battery-operated vehicles from payment
of permit fee.
(iv) Notification issued vide G.S.R. 167(E) dated the 1st March, 2019 to facilitate
retro-fitment of hybrid electric system or electric kit to vehicles.
(v) Notification issued vide G.S.R. 749(E) dated the 7th August 2018 enabling
registration mark of Battery Operated Vehicles in Yellow colour on Green
background for the transport vehicles and for all other cases, in White colour on
Green background.
(vi) Further, an advisory dated 12th August 2020 has been issued to all States
and UTs regarding sale and registration of two-wheeled Electric Vehicles without
batteries.
03.04.2025 835
3. The Government in the Ministry of Heavy Industries (MHI) has formulated
following Schemes to incentivise and promote adoption of Electric Vehicles :
(i) PM Electric Drive Revolution in Innovative Vehicle Enhancement (PM E-
DRIVE) Scheme: PM E-DRIVE Scheme has been notified on 29th September
2024 for promotion of electric mobility and to reduce dependence of fossil fuels in
the country. The scheme has an outlay of ₹10,900 Crore over a period of two
years from 01.04.2024 to 31.03.2026. The Electric Mobility Promotion Scheme
(EMPS) 2024 implemented for the period of 06 months, from 01.04.2024 to
30.09.2024, is subsumed in the PM E-DRIVE scheme. This scheme aims to
incentivise sale of e-2W, e-3W, e-Trucks, e-Ambulances, and ebuses. The
scheme also supports development of charging infrastructure and upgradation of
vehicle testing agencies.
(ii) Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Automobile and Auto Component
Industry (PLI-Auto): Government on 15th September 2021 has approved PLI-Auto
Scheme for enhancing manufacturing capabilities for Advanced Automotive
Technology (AAT) products with a budgetary outlay of ₹25,938 Crore.
(iii) Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for manufacturing Advanced
Chemistry Cells (ACC): Government on 12th May, 2021 approved PLI-ACC in
order to promote manufacturing of ACC in the country with a budgetary outlay of
₹18,100 Crore. The scheme envisages to establish a cumulative ACC battery
manufacturing capacity of 50 GWh.
03.04.2025 836
(iv) Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric Vehicles (FAME)
Scheme Phase-II (FAME-II): FAME-II was implemented for a period of 5 years
with effect from 1st April, 2019 with a total budgetary support of ₹11,500 Crore.
Under FAME-II, Phased Manufacturing Programme (PMP) was introduced with
the objective of domestic manufacturing of electric vehicles, its assemblies/ sub-
assemblies and parts/sub-parts thereby increasing the domestic value addition.
(v) PM e-Bus Sewa-Payment Security Mechanism (PSM) Scheme: This Scheme
notified on 28th October 2024, has an outlay of ₹3,435.33 Crore and aims to
support deployment of more than 38,000 electric buses. The objective of Scheme
is to provide payment security to ebus operators in case of default by Public
Transport Authorities (PTAs).
(vi) Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electric Passenger Cars in India
(SPMEPCI): This scheme was notified on 15th March 2024 to promote the
manufacturing of electric cars in the country. This scheme requires applicants to
invest a minimum of ₹4150 Crore and to achieve a minimum Domestic Value
Addition (DVA) of 25% at the end of the third year and DVA of 50% at the end of
the fifth year.
REVIVAL OF SABEYA AIRFIELD/AIRPORT
5425. DR. ALOK KUMAR SUMAN:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
03.04.2025 837
(a) whether the Government is aware that there is a commercial viability and
heavy traffic demand at the Sabeya (Hathuwa) Airport/Airfield and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has taken any measures for the revival of said
Airport/Airfield;
(c) if so, the details thereof along with the details of the initiative taken for
approving the terminal building design for the revival of the said Airfield and if not,
the reasons therefor;
(d) whether the Government has sent any team for the assessment of the said
Airfield, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(e) whether it is a fact that the Government has not approved/released any
budget for revival of the said Airfield to start operational activity by small aircrafts
and if so, the reasons therefor;
(f) whether the Government has initiated any action plan for the revival of the said
Airfield since its inclusion in the RCS-UDAN Scheme; and
(g) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
03.04.2025 838
(a) to (g): An airport which is included in the awarded routes of UDAN and
requires upgradation/development for commencement of UDAN operations, is
developed under the 'Revival of unserved and underserved airports' scheme. On
development and readiness of the airport, Selected Airline Operators (SAOs)
commence operations on the awarded UDAN routes connecting these airports.
Sabeya (Hathua) airstrip, Bihar is available in the list of unserved airports in the
UDAN Document. None of the airline bidder has submitted the proposal to
operate RCS flight from Sabeya (Hathua) airport till the completion of five bidding
rounds conducted so far. In case, any airline submits a bid for routes connecting
to Sabeya Airstrip in future rounds of bidding, the same will be considered as per
provisions of the UDAN scheme.
PMUY BENEFICIARIES
5426. SHRI VISHNU DAYAL RAM:
SHRI MADHAVANENI RAGHUNANDAN RAO:
SHRI MAHENDRA SINGH SOLANKY:
SHRI NABA CHARAN MAJHI:
SHRIMATI MAHIMA KUMARI MEWAR:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
03.04.2025 839
(a) whether the Government has provided any specific relaxation to migrant
households to get a LPG connection under Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
(PMUY) and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the number of PMUY beneficiaries who have refilled their LPG cylinders
during the last two years; and
(c) the manner in which the PMUY has led to a significant impact on the lives of
rural households, especially women and families in rural and remote areas of
Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh particularly in Dewas Lok Sabha Constituency?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (c): Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched in May, 2016
with an objective to provide deposit free LPG connection to adult women from
poor households across the country. The target to release 8 crore connections
under PMUY was achieved in September 2019. To cover the remaining poor
households, Ujjwala 2.0 was launched in August 2021 with a target to release 1
crore additional PMUY connections, which was achieved in January 2022.
Subsequently, the Government decided to release 60 lakh more LPG connections
under Ujjwala 2.0 and the target of 1.60 crore Ujjwala 2.0 connections was also
achieved during December 2022. Further, Government approved release of
03.04.2025 840
additional 75 lakh connections under PMUY Scheme for the period FY 2023-24 to
2025-26 which has already been achieved during July 2024. As on 01.03.2025,
there are 10.33 crore PMUY connections across the country. Details of LPG
connections, as on 01.03.2025, under PMUY Scheme in Madhya Pradesh and
Rajasthan and Dewas Lok Sabha constituency (which covers Dewas, Sehore,
Agar Malwa and Shajapur district) are at Statement.
LPG connection under PMUY is released in the name of adult women of
the poor households, provided no LPG connection exists in the name of any
family member of the household and fulfilling other terms and conditions. Under
Ujjwala 2.0, a special provision has been made for migrant families who can use
a self-declaration instead of Proof of Address and Ration Card to apply for a
PMUY connection.
Out of the 8.99 crore PMUY connections released as on 01.04.2022, 8.34
crore customers have availed at least one refill during last two financial years i.e.
between April 2022 and March 2024.
Various independent studies and reports have shown that PMUY scheme
has had a significant positive impact on the lives of rural households, especially
women and families in rural and remote areas. Some key benefits are briefly
explained below:
(i) PMUY had resulted in a shift from traditional cooking methods that involve
burning solid fuels like wood, dung and crop residues. The use of cleaner fuel
03.04.2025 841
lowers indoor air pollution, leading to improved respiratory health, particularly
among women and children who are traditionally more exposed to household
smoke.
(ii) Households in rural areas, especially those in remote locations, often spend a
significant portion of their time and energy in collecting traditional cooking fuels.
LPG has reduced drudgery and the time spent on cooking by women of poor
households. The free time, thus, available with them can be utilized in multiple
spheres for enhanced economic productivity.
(iii) Transition from biomass and traditional fuels to LPG reduces the dependency
on wood and other biomass for cooking purposes, leading to a decrease in
deforestation and environmental degradation. This benefits not only the
households but also contributes to broader environmental conservation efforts.
(iv) With improved cooking facilities, there is a potential positive impact on
nutrition. Families may find it easier to cook a variety of nutritious meals,
contributing to better overall health.
STATEMENT
Details of connections released under PMUY Scheme –
As on 01.03.2025
State/UT Number of PMUY Customers
Madhya Pradesh 88,47,087
03.04.2025 842
Rajasthan 73,81,514
Source: Indian Oil Corporation Limited on Industry basis

WILLINGDON ISLAND ROAD CORRIDOR PROJECT
5427. SHRI HIBI EDEN:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has taken any steps
to designate Nettoor as a converging point for four National Highway corridors, if
so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(b) whether discussions between NHAI, the Naval Base and Cochin Port Authority
have successfully resolved land acquisition requirements for the Willingdon Island
03.04.2025 843
corridor and whether any further approvals are needed to proceed with
construction, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(c) whether the Government has finalized the revised alignment and Detailed
Project Report (DPR) for the Willingdon Island road corridor project, if so, the
details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
(d) whether NHAI has decided to establish a cloverleaf intersection at Nettoor on
NH 66 instead of a trumpet flyover to accommodate the projected traffic from the
bypass and three Greenfield National Highways and if so, the details thereof and
if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) to (d): The Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the development of the
Kundanoor - Angamaly road, part of the Ernakulam Bypass project on NH-544, is
currently in progress. The scope of the DPR includes an alignment study,
feasibility study, and pre-construction activities (including land acquisition
assessment). As a part of feasibility study, Nettoor is identified as junction point of
proposed NH-544, existing NH-966B and NH-66.
The alignment options which includes a cloverleaf intersection at Nettoor is
under review in consultation with Defence and Cochin Port Authorities. The
decision regarding construction work shall be taken up after the outcome of DPR,
03.04.2025 844
fulfillment of the criteria, requirement of connectivity, inter-se priority and synergy
with PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
HOAX BOMB THREATS TO AIRLINES
5428. SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has recorded an increase in hoax bomb threats to
airlines in recent years and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the number of individuals arrested in connection with such threats in 2024 and
the legal actions taken against them;
(c) the impact of these threats on airline operations and passenger safety
including any financial implications;
(d) the measures implemented by the Government to prevent and respond to
such hoax bomb threats ensuring minimal disruption to air travel; and
(e) the manner in which these initiatives align with the Government's commitment
to maintaining robust aviation security and public confidence in air travel?
03.04.2025 845
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a): As per Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS), the aviation security
regulator in the country, a total of 836 hoax bomb threats have been received by
Airline Operators since 2022 till 25th March 2025. The details of such threat
messages is as under:;
Year Number of bomb threat received;
2022 13
2023 71
2024 728
2025 (upto 25.03.2025) 24
(b) to (e): 13 people have been arrested in 2024 for making hoax bomb threats to
airlines. BCAS has mandated robust protocols for handling such threats in
coordination with Law Enforcement Agencies, Central Security Agencies, CISF
and stakeholders, leading to minimal impact on flight operations. Bomb Threat
Contingency Plan (BTCP), a detailed contingency plan, is in place to handle such
threats. As a part of BTCP, every airport has a designated Bomb Threat
Assessment Committee (BTAC) which analyses the threat and takes action. In
03.04.2025 846
order to deal with hoax bomb threats, BCAS has issued advisories to all the Civil
Aviation stakeholders in the country to ensure maintenance of robust aviation
security infrastructure and uphold public confidence in air travel.

‘जल’ रा(cid:186)य का िवषय है, इसिलए जल संसाधन(cid:332) के संवध(cid:170)न, संर(cid:177)ण और कुशल (cid:255)बंधन के

संपू(cid:229)रत करने के िलए, क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार िविभ(cid:198)न योजनाओ ं और काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) के मा(cid:197)यम स े उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) तकनीक(cid:236)
और िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान करती है।
क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय भूजल बोड(cid:170) (सीजीड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूबी) ने लगभग 25 लाख वग(cid:170) िकलोमीटर के पूरे मानिचि(cid:253)त (cid:177)े(cid:253)

PROJECTS SANCTIONED UNDER JJM IN KERALA
5431. ADV DEAN KURIAKOSE:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the projects sanctioned under the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) in
Kerala during the last five years and the current year, district-wise;
(b) the process adopted for the selection of projects for special assistance under
JJM in Kerala; and
(c) the number of the projects recommended by the State Government of Kerala
for inclusion under Special Assistance?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V. SOMANNA):
(a) to (c): Since August 2019, Government of India in partnership with States is
implementing Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal to make provision of
potable water to every rural household of the country, including those in all the
districts of Kerala, through functional tap water connection.
At the start of the Mission, only 16.64 lakh (23.51%) rural households were
reported to have tap water connections in Kerala. So far, as reported by the State
as on 31.03.2025, under Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal around 21.92
lakh additional rural households have been provided with tap water connections.
Thus, as on 31.03.2025, out of 70.77 lakh rural households in the State, more
03.04.2025 854
than 38.56 lakh (54.48%) households are reported to have tap water supply in
their homes.
Drinking Water being a State subject, States have been empowered to
plan, design, approve, implement and operate and maintain the drinking water
supply schemes. Government of India supplements the efforts of the States by
providing technical and financial assistance. As per operational guidelines for the
implementation of Centrally Sponsored Programme Jal Jeevan Mission, State
Level Scheme Sanctioning Committee (SLSSC), headed by Chief Secretary/
Addl. Chief Secretary/ Principal Secretary/ Secretary in-charge of Public Health
Engineering (PHE)/ Rural Water Supply (RWS) Department, approves the
schemes/ projects being taken up under JJM. As such, district-wise details of
individual projects/ schemes for rural water supply projects are not maintained at
the Government of India level.
RECRUITMENT IN CIVIL AVIAITION SECTOR
5432. SHRI TANGELLA UDAY SRINIVAS :
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) the details of the total sanctioned strength, number of personnel in position
and number of vacancies in the Directorate General of Civil Aviation, Bureau of
03.04.2025 855
Civil Aviation Security and Airports Authority of India during the last five years and
the current year, year-wise;
(b) the number of vacancies filled in these organizations during the said period,
year-wise;
(c) the specific recruitment target set by the Government for filling vacancies in
the financial year 2025-26 along with the timeline fixed for achieving the same;
(d) the details of the funds allocated and utilised for recruitment and manpower
expansion in these organizations during the said period, year-wise;
(e) the details of the budgetary provisions made under the Union Budget 2025-26
for recruitment-related expenses;
(f) whether the said provisions adequately reflect the Government’s plan to fill
these vacancies and if so, the details thereof along with the steps taken to
expedite the recruitment process; and
(g) whether any special drives, contractual appointments and alternative hiring
mechanisms are being considered to address manpower shortages and if so, the
details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
03.04.2025 856
(a): The details of the total sanctioned strength, number of personnel in position
and number of vacancies in the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA),
Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) and Airports Authority of India (AAI)
during the last five years is enclosed as Statement-I.
(b): The number of vacancies filled in the respective organization during the last
five years is given at Statement-II.
(c): In DGCA, 441 posts have been created in the restructuring exercise finalized
during 2022 and 2024 (416 created in 2022 and 2023 and 25 in 2024). The
process of filling up involves framing of recruitment rules and the action by the
recruiting agencies like Union Public Services Commission (UPSC) and Staff
Selection Commission (SSC). The recruitment is a continuous process and posts
are filled up according to the prescribed procedure.
BCAS is a deputation based organisation wherein personnel are recruited
on deputation basis from other departments and is a dynamic process.;
AAI has tentatively planned 1098 Executive level recruitment for FY 2025-
26 under the Mission Mode Recruitment of Government, which are likely to be
completed as per following schedule:;
(i) 66 appointments in August, 2025;
(ii) 723 in October 2025, and ;
03.04.2025 857
(iii) 309 in November 2025.;
(d) to (f): In DGCA and BCAS, no separate funds are earmarked for recruitment of
persons. The persons recruited are paid out of budgetary allocations given to the
Department at various stages of Budget Estimates, Revised Estimates and
through Supplementary. The recruitment is conducted every year based on
vacancies through UPSC, SSC and on deputation basis. The details of the funds
allocated and utilized for salaries, professional services and allowances in respect
of DGCA and BCAS during the last five years are attached as Statement-III.
AAI has made a budgetary provision (Budget Estimates) for F.Y. 2025-26
for recruitment and manpower expansion of Rs.61.48 Crores. Based on tentative
recruitment plan, estimated budget provisions are accordingly made.The details
of the funds allocated and utilized for recruitment and manpower expansion in AAI
during the last five years are attached as Statement-IV.
(g): In DGCA and BCAS, contractual appointments and alternative methods of
hiring are resorted to based on provisions contained in relevant rules and the
extant government policy applicable from time to time. In DGCA, total number of
107 consultants/Young Professional/FOIs on contract basis, and in BCAS, total of
14 consultants were hired to address manpower shortages.
AAI awards certain works on contract basis under the provisions of the
Contract Labour (Regulation and Abolition) Act, 1970 in the activities which are
03.04.2025 858
sporadic in nature such as Bird Scaring, Security Services, and some of
maintenance activities through outsourcing model on need basis.

AVERAGE ENERGY EFFICIENCY OF TPPS
5433. SHRI PUTTA MAHESH KUMAR:
SHRI MAGUNTA SREENIVASULU REDDY:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has conducted any study/survey regarding the
energy efficiency of Thermal Power Plants (TPPs) in the country during the last
five years;
03.04.2025 864
(b) if so, the details and the average energy efficiency thereof;
(c) the details of the steps taken/being taken to increase energy efficiency and
output of thermal power plants across the country especially in Andhra Pradesh;
and
(d) whether the Government has considered for upgrading the existing technology
in thermal power plants to increase efficiency across the country and if so, the
details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER OF
STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) and (b): As per the report of Central Electricity Authority, the all India thermal
efficiency of coal and lignite based plants is 35.02 % in the year 2022-23. The all
India thermal Efficiency of Coal and Lignite Based Plants for the years 2018-19
to 2022-23 are given below:
Year Efficiency (%)
2018-19 35.62
2019-20 35.69
2020-21 35.88
2021-22 35.11
2022-23 35.02
03.04.2025 865
(c) and (d) : To improve the energy efficiency in Thermal Power Plants (TPPs),
the TPP sector has been included under the PAT scheme. This scheme stipulates
improvement in the heat rate of TPPs in a cycle of 3 years. This scheme covers a
total capacity of 184 GW including 11.2 GW capacity of TPPs in Andhra Pradesh.
As a result of implementation of the scheme, the TPP sector has achieved energy
savings of 7.73 million tonnes of oil equivalent (MToE) including 0.146 MToE in
Andhra Pradesh.
Power generation is a de-licensed activity and promoters of TPPs take conscious
decision in regard to suitable technologies for their TPPs. However, Ministry of
Power encourages installation of efficient Super-critical/ Ultra Super-critical units
over sub-critical coal based thermal units as these units are more efficient than
sub-critical units.
As on 28.03.2025, 98 super critical units totaling to 68,070 MW and 8 Ultra
Supercritical units totaling to 5,560 MW have been installed in the country
including 8 super critical units with total capacity of 5,840 MW in Andhra Pradesh.
RENTAL HOUSING FOR INDUSTRIAL WORKERS
5434. SHRI SRIBHARAT MATHUKUMILLI:
03.04.2025 866
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the dormitory-type rental housing scheme for industrial workers has
been implemented and if so, the details thereof, location-wise;
(b) the details of the funds sanctioned along with the progress made under the
said scheme, particularly in industrial clusters including those in Andhra Pradesh;
(c) whether the participation of the private sector in the said scheme has been
limited due to regulatory hurdles such as low Floor Area Ratio (FAR) and Ground
Coverage Ratio (GCR) compared to international standards which restrict both
horizontal and vertical expansion and if so, the details thereof;
(d) the measures taken/being taken by the Government to address these
regulatory hurdles and encourage greater private-sector participation in the said
scheme; and
(e) whether the Government proposes to introduce any policy and regulatory
reforms as highlighted by NITI Aayog to integrate workers' accommodation into
industrial planning, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (e): Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) launched Affordable
Rental Housing Complexes (ARHCs) as a sub-scheme of Pradhan Mantri Awas
03.04.2025 867
Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) to provide dignified living to urban migrants/poor
including industrial workers near their workplace through single/double bedroom
units or dormitory beds in the country, including Andhra Pradesh. This scheme is
implemented through two models:
i.Model-1: Utilizing existing Government funded vacant houses constructed under
Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission (JNNURM) and Rajiv Awas
Yojana (RAY) to convert into ARHCs through Public Private Partnership (PPP) or
by Public Agencies,
ii.Model-2: Construction, Operation and Maintenance of ARHCs by Public/Private
Entities on their own available vacant land.
Beneficiaries of ARHCs are urban migrants/poor from Economically Weaker
Section (EWS)/Low Income Group (LIG). They include labour, urban poor (street
vendors, rickshaw pullers, other service providers etc.), industrial workers, and
migrants working with market/trade associations, educational/health institutions,
hospitality sector, long term tourists/visitors, students or any other persons of
such category.
So far, no proposal under ARHCs has been received from the State
Government of Andhra Pradesh. The State/UT-wise number of ARHCs
sanctioned, completed under both models along with the details of Technology
03.04.2025 868
Innovation Grant sanctioned under Model-2 by the Ministry in the country are at
Statement.
The scheme has witnessed active participation of Private Sector in Model-
2. As per the Scheme Guidelines, public/private entities are encouraged to invest
in the rental housing sector and develop ARHCs through various
benefits/incentives provided by Governments.
Further, In the Union Budget 2024-25, Government has announced rental
housing with dormitory-type accommodation for industrial workers to be facilitated
in public-private partnership (PPP) mode with Viability Gap Funding (VGF)
support and commitment from anchor industries. For this purpose, ₹2,500 crore
has been provisioned in 2025-26 budget.
Further, learning from the experiences of 9 years implementation of PMAY-U,
MoHUA has launched PMAY-U 2.0 ‘Housing for All’ Mission with effect from
01.09.2024 for implementation in urban areas across the country to construct,
purchase and rent a house by eligible beneficiaries at affordable cost through four
verticals i.e., Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in
Partnership (AHP), Affordable Rental Housing (ARH) and Interest Subsidy
Scheme (ISS). The scheme guidelines are available at https://pmay-
urban.gov.in/uploads/guidelines/Operational-Guidelines-of-PMAY-U-2.pdf.
03.04.2025 869
ARH vertical of PMAY-U 2.0 aims to create a conducive environment by
incentivizing Public/Private Entities to leverage investment for creating affordable
rental housing stock. It promotes the creation of adequate rental housing for
eligible EWS/LIG beneficiaries including industrial workers who do not want to
own a house but require housing for short term basis. As per scheme guidelines
of PMAY-U 2.0, Public/Private Entities can construct, operate and maintain ARH
projects containing Single/Double bedroom unit or dormitory beds to provide
accommodation for eligible beneficiaries.

DRINKING WATER PROBLEM IN KUTTANAD
5435. SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the severe drinking water crisis due to
increasing salinity, pollution and inadequate infrastructure in Kuttanad, Kerala;
(b) if so, the details thereof along with the details of any ongoing or proposed
central assistance for improving drinking water availability in the region;
(c) whether the Government has received any proposals from the State
Government of Kerala for desalination plants, pipeline infrastructure or other
water supply projects for Kuttanad;
(d) if so, the details and status thereof along with the estimated timeline for
implementation; and
(e) whether the Government would consider a special drinking water package for
Kuttanad under the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) or any other central scheme and if
so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V. SOMANNA):
03.04.2025 872
(a) to (e): Since August 2019, Government of India in partnership with States is
implementing Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal to make provision of
potable water to every rural household of the country, including those in the
Kuttanad, Alappuzha district of Kerala, through functional tap water connection
i.e. at a service level of 55 litre per capita per day (lpcd), of prescribed quality
(BIS:10500), on regular and long-term basis.
At the start of the Mission, only 1.85 lakh (33.61%) rural households were
reported to have tap water connections in Alappuzha. So far, as reported by the
State as on 31.03.2025, under Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal around
1.80 lakh additional rural households have been provided with tap water
connections. Thus, as on 31.03.2025, out of 5.52 lakh rural households in the
Alappuzha district of Kerala, more than 3.65 lakh (66.20%) households are
reported to have tap water supply in their homes.
To achieve 100 per cent coverage through continued implementation of
mission, Hon'ble Finance Minister during her budget speech 2025-26 has
announced extension of Jal Jeevan Mission until 2028 with enhanced total outlay.
Drinking Water being a State subject, the responsibility of planning, approval,
implementation, operation, and maintenance of drinking water supply schemes,
including those under the Jal Jeevan Mission, lies with State/UT Governments.
The Government of India supports the States by providing technical and financial
assistance. The choice of technology, best suited to the context in a State/ UT,
03.04.2025 873
including use of desalination, for making provision of potable water supply to
households lies with the respective State/UT Governments. Further, as per
operational guidelines for the implementation of Centrally Sponsored Programme
Jal Jeevan Mission, State Level Scheme Sanctioning Committee (SLSSC),
headed by Chief Secretary/ Addl. Chief Secretary/ Principal Secretary/ Secretary
in-charge of Public Health Engineering (PHE)/ Rural Water Supply (RWS)
Department, approves the schemes/ projects being taken up under JJM.
Numerous projects inter alia including projects aiming at strengthening
water storage, distribution systems, or purification facilities are implemented
concurrently at various levels across the States for achieving the envisaged
objectives. As such, project-wise details of individual projects/ schemes for rural
water supply projects are not maintained at the Government of India level.

PROJECTS UNDER AIBP IN ANDHRA PRADESH AND TELANGANA
5437. SHRI Y. S. AVINASH REDDY:
SHRI RAMASAHAYAM RAGHURAM REDDY:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the details of projects that are planned to be supported through the
Accelerated Irrigation Benefit Programme (AIBP) and other sources of funding
including the priority regions identified for irrigation development; and
(b) whether any ongoing or new irrigation projects in Andhra Pradesh and
Telangana have receive financial support under the AIBP and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
03.04.2025 876
(a) :Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) was launched during the
year 2015-16, with an aim to enhance physical access of water on farm and
expand cultivable area under assured irrigation, improve on-farm water use
efficiency, introduce sustainable water conservation practices, etc. Accelerated
Irrigation Benefits Programme component of PMKSY is mainly for expeditious
completion of major and medium irrigation projects.
Total 106 nos. ongoing Major/Medium Irrigation Projects (MMI) were
identified, in consultation with States, under PMKSY-AIBP during 2016-17. Out of
these, 63 nos. projects have been completed and 43 nos. projects are ongoing.
Details of 106 nos. projects are given at Statement-I. Further, details of new
projects included under PMKSY-AIBP and other projects are given at Statement-
II. Under, Special Package for Maharashtra central assistance is being provided
to 8 nos. Major/ Medium Irrigation (MMI) projects in drought prone districts in
Vidarbha and Marathwada regions and rest of Maharashtra. List of projects
under Special Package for Maharashtra is placed as Statement-III.
(b): Yes, 7 nos. ongoing irrigation projects in Andhra Pradesh and 11 nos.
ongoing irrigation projects in Telangana are getting central assistance under
PMKSY-AIBP scheme. The details of projects in Andhra Pradesh are given in
Statement-IV and the details of projects in Telangana are given in Statement-V.
03.04.2025 877

ALLOCATION OF FUNDS UNDER TRIBAL SUB PLAN
5438. DR. GUMMA THANUJA RANI:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the funds allocated under the Tribal Sub Plan component for
various schemes implemented by the Department of Fisheries since 2019-20 in
the country, State-wise including Andhra Pradesh;
(b) whether the allocation of the funds for Andhra Pradesh was zero in 2020-21
and if so, the reasons therefor;
03.04.2025 887
(c) whether the Government is aware that Andhra Pradesh has a sizeable tribal
population, if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government has taken this into account and urged the utilization
of the funds under the Tribal Sub Plan component and if so, the details thereof;
(e) whether the Government has received any representation from the State
Government of Andhra Pradesh regarding the utilization of the funds under this
component in 2020-21 and if so, the details thereof; and
(f) the reasons for denial of the funds, if any?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a) and (b): State-wise details of funds including Andhra Pradesh allocated under
the Tribal Sub Plan/Development Action Plan for Scheduled Tribes (DAPST) by
the Department of Fisheries since 2019-20 is given at Statement-I. Rs. 655.38
lakhs were released to Andhra Pradesh by Department of Fisheries in 2020-21
under DAPST.
(c) :As per Census 2011, Andhra Pradesh has a total population of 4,93,86,799
out of which 26,31,145 are Scheduled Tribes which is approximately 5.3% of
State’s population.
03.04.2025 888
(d) :Ministry of Tribal Affairs is implementing various schemes/programmes for the
welfare and development of the Scheduled Tribes (STs) in the country. Details of
funds released to Andhra Pradesh under the schemes of Ministry during the last
four years is given at Statement-II.
Ministry regularly communicates with State Governments to ensure utilization of
funds released to them. Ministry convenes meetings with State Government
officials for ensuring timely submission of proposals, speeding up of
implementation of the schemes, and reviewing the physical and financial
progress.
(e) and (f): DAPST fund was released to Andhra Pradesh in 2020-21.

SHORTAGE OF DRINKING WATER IN RURAL AREAS OF RAJASTHAN
5439. SHRI KULDEEP INDORA:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken any special initiatives through its various
schemes to ensure that rural areas of water deficient districts of Rajasthan get
sufficient and safe supply of drinking water;
(b) if so, the details thereof; and
(c) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure that the rural areas
of Sriganganagar and Hanumangarh districts get safe drinking water during the
last three years and the current year?
03.04.2025 893
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V. SOMANNA):
(a) to (c): Since August 2019, Government of India is implementing Jal Jeevan
Mission (JJM), in partnership with States/ UTs, including water deficient districts
of Rajasthan, to make provision of tap water supply to every rural household of
the country in adequate quantity (55lpcd) of prescribed quality (BIS:10500) on
long term basis. Drinking water is a state subject, and hence, the responsibility of
planning, approval, implementation, operation, and maintenance of drinking water
supply schemes, including those under the JJM, lies with State/UT Governments.
The Government of India supports the States by providing technical and financial
assistance.
As reported by the state government of Rajasthan on JJM IMIS, on
15.08.2019, only 11.68 lakh (10.85%) rural households had tap water
connections. Since then, around 48.77 lakh additional rural households have
been provided with tap water connections under JJM. Thus, as on 31.03.2025,
out of 107.75 lakh rural households in state, the provision of tap water supply is
available to approximately 60.46 lakh (56.12%) rural households.
As reported by state on JJM-IMIS, in Sriganganagar district, on 15.08.2019,
12,926 (7.01%) rural households had tap water connections. Since then, 1,55,139
additional rural households have been provided with tap water connections under
JJM. Thus, as on 31.03.2025, out of 1,84,311 rural households, the provision of
tap water supply is available to 1,68,065 (91.19%) rural households in the district.
03.04.2025 894
Similarly, in Hanumangarh district, on 15.08.2019, 77,784 (26.61%) rural
households had tap water connections. Since then, 1,74,330 additional rural
households have been provided with tap water connections under JJM. Thus, as
on 31.03.2025, out of 2,92,298 rural households, the provision of tap water supply
is available to 2,52,114 (86.25%) rural households in the district.
CLUSTER-BASED MSMEs DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMMES
5440. DR. SANJAY JAISWAL:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Ministry is implementing cluster-based MSME development
programmes in backward regions like West Champaran;
(b) if so, the details of such clusters established in the district during the last three
years and the industries they focus on;
(c) whether there are plans to set up business incubation centers in West
Champaran to promote entrepreneurship and innovation;
(d) if so, the expected timeline and the type of support (financial, technical and
marketing) these centers would provide; and
03.04.2025 895
(e) the steps being taken to link MSMEs in West Champaran with national and
international e- commerce platforms to expand their market reach and improve
competitiveness?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b): Ministry of MSME is implementing Micro and Small Enterprises
Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP) scheme across the country. The
objective of the MSE-CDP scheme is to enhance the sustainability,
competitiveness and growth of Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by
addressing common issues such as improvement of technology, skill and quality
etc. for their holistic development adopting cluster development approach by
extending financial assistance as GoI grant to establish Common Facility Centers
(CFCs) in the existing clusters and establish/up-grade infrastructural facilities in
new/existing industrial estates/areas/flatted factory complexes. It is a demand
driven scheme and proposals under the scheme are received from the State/UT
Governments as per their need. No proposal from West Champaran under MSE-
CDP has been received so far.
(c) and (d): Ministry of MSME is also implementing the Incubation component of
MSME Innovative Scheme under MSME Champions Scheme. This provides
opportunity to Institutions such as Technical Colleges, Universities, other
03.04.2025 896
Professional Colleges/Institutes, Rand D Institutes, NGOs involved in incubation
activities, MSME-DIs/ Technology Centres or any Institute/ Organization of
Central/ State Government to apply for registration as a Host Institute (HI) and act
as a Business Incubator (BI). It is a demand driven scheme and no proposal for
the registration of Host Institutions (HIs) / Business Incubators (BIs) under
Incubation component of MSME Innovative scheme has been received so far
from West Champaran district.
(e): Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) is an initiative of Department for
Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and
Industry, Government of India, aimed to democratize digital commerce and
enable small businesses to capitalize on the benefits of digital commerce by
creating a level playing field. This initiative provides equal opportunities to small
businesses including MSMEs, artisans, and self-help groups (SHGs) to leverage
digital commerce and expand their market reach. The Ministry of MSME has
launched a MSME Trade Enablement and Marketing (TEAM) Scheme, designed
to onboard 5 lakh Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) onto ONDC.
FUNDS TO PROMOTE FPI IN BHIWANI-MAHENDRAGARH
5441. SHRI DHARAMBIR SINGH:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
03.04.2025 897
(a) whether the Government has allocated any funds to promote food processing
industries in Bhiwani-Mahendragarh;
(b) if so, the details of the funds allocated and projects implemented during the
last three years and the current year;
(c) whether the Government plans to establish mega food parks and cold storage
facilities in this region;
(d) if so, the status of these projects and their beneficiaries; and
(e) whether the Government provides any special grants or concessions to agri-
based startups and MSMEs, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) to (d) : In order to promote and ensure overall development of Food
Processing Industries, Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has been
incentivizing setting up/ expansion of related infrastructure through its Central
Sector “Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY) Scheme”, Central
Sector “Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry
(PLISFPI)” and Centrally sponsored “PM Formalization of Micro Food Processing
Enterprises (PMFME)” Scheme across the country including Bhiwani and
03.04.2025 898
Mahendragarh districts of Haryana. These schemes are not region specific but
demand driven.
Under PMKSY, credit linked financial assistance (capital subsidy) is
provided to entrepreneurs for setting up of food processing industries with total
outlay of Rs 5520 Cr for 15th Finance Commission Cycle.
Under PMFME scheme, financial, technical and business support is
provided for setting up / upgradation of micro food processing enterprises. The
scheme is operational for a period of upto 2025-26 with an outlay of Rs.10,000
Crore.
PLISFPI is, inter alia, intended to support creation of global food
manufacturing champions and support Indian brands of food products in
international market. The Scheme is operational for a period from 2021-22 to
2026-27 with an outlay of Rs. 10,900 crores.
Being central sector schemes, no state-wise budget allocation is done
under PMKSY and PLISFPI. Year-wise total allocation and funds utilized under
PMKSY and PLISFPI since 2021-22 and funds released under PMFME schemes
in Haryana State since 2021-22 are at Statement-I and the details of projects
approved under these schemes in Haryana State since 2021-22 is placed at
Statement-II.
MoFPI does not set up food processing industries on its own. However, it
incentivizes setting up of these Industries through Grants to entrepreneurs. The
Mega Food Park Scheme has been discontinued by the Ministry since
03.04.2025 899
01.04.2021. MoFPI, under its schemes, does not provide financial assistance for
establishment of standalone cold storages facilities. However, it incentivizes
creation of cold storage infrastructure as part of food processing projects under
relevant component schemes of PMKSY.
Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is implementing Mission
for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH) under which capital assistance
is provided for various horticulture activities including Construction/ Expansion/
Modernization of cold storages of capacity up to 5000 MT in the country on the
basis of Annual Action Plan (AAP) received from States/UTs.
(e): Ministry extends financial support to prospective entrepreneurs including agri-
based startups for setting up of different kinds of food processing industries, as
per respective scheme guidelines. Entrepreneurs in the Micro, Small and
Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector are also incentivized under the schemes of
MoFPI. The details of incentives available under these schemes are at
Statement-III.

03.04.2025 905
Incentives available under Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food
Processing Industry (PLIS-FPI)
i.The beneficiary should achieve minimum year on year sales growth of 10% for
claiming incentive under Category-I, Category-II and Millet-Based Products
components of the Scheme. Under Category -I component, the companies have
to make committed investments to increase their production capacities. If a
company does not make the committed investment upto end of 2023-24, it is not
eligible to receive incentives under the Scheme.
ii.Under Category-III, i.e., Branding and Marketing component, a company is
eligible for financial incentives @ 50% of expenditure incurred on Branding and
Marketing abroad subject to a maximum of 3% of Sales of food products or Rs 50
crore per year, whichever is less. The minimum expenditure should be Rs. 5 crore
over a period of five years.
The details of assistance available to Micro Food Processing Enterprises
under PMFME Scheme
(i). Support to Individual / Group Category Micro Enterprises: Credit-linked capital
subsidy @35% of the eligible project cost, maximum ceiling Rs.10 lakh per unit;
(ii). Support to SHGs for seed capital: Seed capital @ Rs. 40,000/- per member of
SHG engaged in food processing for working capital and purchase of small tools
subject to maximum of Rs. 4 lakh per SHG Federation.
(iii). Support for Common Infrastructure: Credit linked capital subsidy @35%
subject to maximum of Rs. 3 crore to support FPOs, SHGs, Cooperatives and any
03.04.2025 906
Government agency for setting up of common infrastructure. The common
infrastructure will also be available for other units and public to utilize on hiring
basis for substantial part of the capacity.
(iv). Branding and Marketing Support: Grant upto 50% for Branding and Marketing
to groups of FPOs/ SHGs/ Cooperatives or an SPV of micro food processing
enterprises.
(v). Capacity Building: The scheme envisages training for Entrepreneurship
Development Skilling (EDP+): program modified to meet the requirement of food
processing industry and product specific skilling.
MAKE IN INDIA INITIATIVE FOR MSMEs
5442. SHRI BIDYUT BARAN MAHATO:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) the measures and strategies taken by the Government to enhance the growth
of MSMEs, especially in fostering innovation, improving ease of doing business,
and facilitating access to new markets; and
(b) the manner in which the "Make in India" initiative is being integrated into the
MSME sector and the steps being taken to promote indigenous manufacturing,
03.04.2025 907
reduce dependence on imports and create new opportunities for MSMEs under
this initiative?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b): Government has taken a number of measures to enhance the growth
of MSMEs, which includes measures for fostering innovation, improving ease of
doing business, and facilitating access to new markets.
The Ministry of MSME implements the MSME Champions Scheme aimed at
modernizing MSMEs by enhancing productivity, reducing wastages, improving
competitiveness, and facilitating their national and global reach. This Scheme
comprises three components: i) MSME-Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme,
ii) MSME-Competitive (Lean) Scheme and iii) MSME-Innovative (Incubation,
Design and IPR) Scheme.
The MSME-Innovative Scheme fosters innovation through a combination of
innovation in incubation, design intervention and by protecting IPR in a single
mode approach to create awareness amongst MSMEs about India’s innovation
and motivate them to become MSME Champions.
To improve ease of doing business for MSMEs, a number of initiatives have been
taken which are as given below:
03.04.2025 908
i.New revised criteria for classification of MSMEs, based on Investment and
Turnover, adopted to widen the ambit of the MSME sector.
ii.“Udyam Registration” for MSMEs launched for Ease of Doing Business.
iii.Ministry of MSME launched Udyam Assist Platform, for bringing Informal Micro
Enterprises (IMEs), in the formal ambit. Registered IMEs can avail the benefits of
Priority Sector Lending.
iv.Non-tax benefits extended for 3 years in case of an upward change in status of
MSMEs.
v.Ministry of MSME has launched Samadhaan Portal for filing of grievances and
monitoring of outstanding dues to the MSEs from the buyers of goods and
services.
vi.Launching of an online Portal “Champions” in June, 2020 to cover many
aspects of e-governance including redressing grievances and handholding of
MSMEs.
To provide an assured market share to Micro and Small Enterprises
(MSEs), Procurement Policy for Micro and Small Enterprises Order, 2012 has
been issued. Procurement and Marketing Support Scheme (PMSS) extends
benefits to MSEs for gaining market access through participation in Trade Fairs/
Exhibitions, Vendor Development Programmes, adoption of Modern Packaging
Technique, e-Commerce platforms, etc.
Under the International Cooperation (IC) Scheme, financial assistance is
provided on reimbursement basis to the eligible Central/State Government
03.04.2025 909
organizations and Industry Associations to facilitate participation of MSMEs in the
international exhibitions/fairs/buyer-seller meets held abroad and for organizing
international conference/seminar/workshops in the country.
As informed by DPIIT, Make in India' Initiative was launched on 25th
September, 2014 to facilitate Investment, foster Innovation, build best in class
Infrastructure, and make India a hub for manufacturing, design, and innovation.
Presently, Make in India 2.0 focuses on 27 sectors implemented across various
Ministries/Departments and State Governments. One of the significant measures
taken up by the Government keeping in view India's vision of becoming
'Atmanirbhar', is the launch of Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Schemes for 14
key sectors to enhance India's Manufacturing capabilities and Exports.
The Ministry of MSME, in order to help the MSMEs to grow technologically
and to enhance their competitiveness, has established new Technology Centres
(TCs) and Extension Centres (ECs) across the country. These TCs/ECs provide
various services like technology support, skilling, incubation and consultancy to
MSMEs and skill seeker, which makes them capable to manufacture in the
country in addition to reducing the dependence on import and create new
opportunities. To digitally empower MSEs, the Trade Enablement and Marketing
(TEAM) Scheme has been launched.
03.04.2025 910
बरले ी म(cid:164) भूजल सरं (cid:177)ण के िलए िवशेष योजना

03.04.2025 912
COASTAL HIGHWAYS
5444. SHRI PARSHOTTAMBHAI RUPALA:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the coastal highways planned and under progress along the
country's coastline, including the route alignment, length and estimated cost of
each project, State-wise including Gujarat;
(b) the measures taken to ensure the environmental sustainability and social
responsibility of the coastal highway projects including the conduct of
environmental impact assessments and the relocation of affected communities;
(c) the allocation of funds for coastal highway projects in the current financial
year;
(d) the timeline set by the Government for the completion of the coastal highway
projects; and
(e) the impact of the coastal highway projects on the economic development,
tourism and connectivity of coastal regions particularly in the State of Gujarat?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
03.04.2025 913
(a): The Government in Ministry of Road Transport and Highways is primarily
responsible for development and maintenance of National Highways (NHs).
Development and maintenance of NHs, including NHs in coastal areas, is a
continuous process. Works on NHs are accordingly taken up based on the traffic
density, condition of the road, inter-se priority, availability of funds and synergy
with PM GatiShakti National Master Plan (NMP).
At present, 27 NHs projects in 1,025 km length costing ₹65,111 crore are
under construction in the country for improving accessibility and connectivity to
the mainland coastline. State / Union Territory (UT) –wise details of these
projects, including for the State of Gujarat, are at Statement.
Further, Government has taken up preparation of Detailed Project Report
(DPR) for development of NHs in 890 km length for improving connectivity to the
mainland coastline, including 67 km long stretch from Narayan Sarovar to Nalia
and 30 km long stretch from Pipaliya to Maliya in the State of Gujarat.
(b): Environmental and Social Impact Assessment and mitigation measures are
part of Detailed Project Report (DPR) of all NHs projects, including NHs in coastal
areas. Further, all NHs development projects are planned in line with PM
GatiShakti National Master Plan (NMP) Principles. The DPR module tool
developed by the Government along with the available features in the dynamic
Geographic Information System (GIS) platform based PM Gati Shakti NMP Portal
is helpful for optimizing the NHs project alignments and planning of projects
considering information related to existing physical / geographical features, forest
03.04.2025 914
areas, eco-sensitive areas, etc., in minimizing ecological impacts, requirements of
clearances and faster and more effective project preparation. In addition, all
projects costing more than ₹500 crore are consulted through Network Planning
Group (NPG) at the Detailed Project Report (DPR) stage itself, having
representation from all the stakeholders including NITI Aayog, and the advice of
NPG is suitably incorporated.
(c): Budgetary allocation for development and maintenance of NHs, including NHs
in coastal areas, during the FY 2024-25 is ₹2,90,245 crore.
(d): At present, 27 NHs projects in 1,025 km length costing ₹65,111 crore are
under construction in the country for improving accessibility and connectivity to
the mainland coastline, with completion in a phased manner by FY 2026-27.
(e): Infrastructure sector is the prime mover of the economy and contributes to the
faster economic growth and development. Government has taken up
development of NHs, including capacity augmentation and development of Way
Side Amenities at approximate intervals of 40-60 km along NHs, for improving
accessibility and connectivity to the mainland coastline, including for the State of
Gujarat, thereby reducing transportation cost and time for movement of goods
and passengers to and from ports and industrial clusters for enhanced trade and
logistics efficiency, besides providing boost to coastal tourism.
03.04.2025 915
STATEMENT
State / UT – wise details of NHs projects under construction in the country,
including for the State of Gujarat, for improving accessibility and
connectivity to the mainland coastline: -
Sl. No. State Nos. Length (in km) Cost (₹ Crore)
1 Andhra Pradesh 2 90 1,128
2 Goa 1 71 952
3 Gujarat 2 110 1,298
4 Kerala 16 522 52,575
5 Maharashtra 3 104 3,782
6 Tamil Nadu 3 128 5,376

CREATE FOR SUPPORT TO RURAL ENTERPRISES
5447. SHRI MUKESHKUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Centre for Rural Enterprise Acceleration through Technology
(CREATE) has been set up to support rural enterprises, if so, the details thereof;
(b) the key facilities and training programme provided by CREATE;
(c) the manner in which the initiative aims to enhance local productivity and
improve the quality of products in the region;
(d) the estimated impact of CREATE on improving the livelihoods of local
communities and enabling artisans to earn higher remuneration; and
(e) whether the Government plans to establish similar centres in other regions to
promote rural enterprise development and economic growth particularly in
Gujarat?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
03.04.2025 919
(a) :The Centre for Rural Enterprise Acceleration through Technology (CREATE)
has been set up at Leh to support rural enterprises, enhance local productivity,
product quality and improve livelihoods of the local traditional artisans.
(b) :The Centre for Rural Enterprise Acceleration through Technology (CREATE)
is a Pashmina wool processing unit which has been set up for conversion of
dehaired Pashmina to Pashmina Roving.
(c) and (d): Before establishment of the CREATE, no Pashmina wool roving
processing facility was available in Leh, and therefore the same was being done
from units based in different states. The setting up of CREATE makes availability
of Pashmina roving facility, locally at Leh, leading to high productivity, consistent
product quality due to automation and high earnings for the artisans. It also
reduces wastage of costly Pashmina fibre.
Increased productivity will lead to increase of earnings of spinners and weavers.
It, further, helps in conserving the art of Pashmina spinning and weaving.
(e): As of now, KVIC has no plan to establish similar centre in Gujarat State.
PLI SCHEME FOR DRONE MANUFACTURING
5448. SUSHRI SAYANI GHOSH:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
03.04.2025 920
(a) whether the Government proposes to introduce a new PLI Scheme for
manufacturers of drones and drone components in the country and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) the details of the achievements of the first PLI Scheme for the said
manufacturers under which an outlay incentive of Rs 120 crores was set aside by
the Government in September 2021;
(c) the reasons for the discontinuation of the said PLI Scheme;
(d) whether the Government is aware that 90% of flight controllers used in small
drones in the country are imported from China; and
(e) if so, the details thereof along with the steps taken by the Government for
indegenisation of drone components?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): The scheme of Production Linked Incentive for drones and drone
components of Ministry of Civil Aviation was notified on 30th September, 2021
with a total incentive of Rs 120 crores spread over three Financial Years (FYs),
FY 2021-22 to FY 2023-24. The Scheme period is now over. Under the scheme a
total amount of Rs 98.32 crores (approx.) was disbursed.
03.04.2025 921
(d) and (e) :As per the available data submitted by Unmanned Aircraft System
(UAS) manufacturers in India with respect to Flight controllers installed in Small
class UAS model Type Certified by Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)
and registered with Unique Identification Numbers as on date, 39 percent of Flight
Controllers have been sourced from manufacturers based in China. The PLI
Scheme of the Ministry included the indigenous manufacture of drone
components.

5449. SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:

mission and declared ‘Smart Cities’ along with the details of the projects
completed so far, city-wise; and
(c) the details of the cities where projects have not been completed under the said
mission along with the details of the incomplete projects, city-wise?
03.04.2025 922
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):

extended the Smart Cities Mission (SCM) up to 31.03.2025 for completion of its
ongoing projects.
(b) and (c): As on 04.03.2025, 7,504 projects (93% of total projects) amounting to
₹1,50,306 crore have been completed and 559 projects amounting to ₹14,239
crore are ongoing in the 100 cities selected under SCM. The city-wise details of
projects completed and ongoing is at Statement.

03.04.2025 930
GROUND WATER DEPLETION CRISIS
5450. SHRI SUKHDEO BHAGAT:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Central Ground Water Board’s report, 2023 indicates that 256 out
of 755 districts in India are facing critical or overexploited ground water levels and
if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether NITI Aayog’s Composite Water Management Index (CWMI) warns
that 40% of India’s population may have no access to drinking water by 2030 and
if so, the details thereof along with the steps taken/being taken by the
Government to address this issue;
(c) the reasons for Government’s failure to effectively curb groundwater depletion
despite the implementation of schemes like Atal Bhujal Yojana;
(d) whether the Government acknowledges that 85% of rural drinking water
supply relies on groundwater and if so, the details thereof along with the
measures taken/being taken to ensure its sustainability; and
(e) whether the Government proposes to enforce stricter regulations for ground
water extraction instead of relying on voluntary compliance and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
03.04.2025 931
(a): In the Dynamic Ground Water Resource Assessment-2023 report prepared
by the Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), 705 districts in the country have
been assessed, out of which 24 districts were under the ‘Critical’ category and 98
districts were under ‘Over-Exploited’ category. The details of such districts is
provided in Statement.
(b): Drinking water is a state subject. However, Government of India is
implementing the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), since August 2019, with an objective
to provide safe and potable tap water supply in adequate quantity, of prescribed
quality and on a regular and long-term basis to all rural households in the
country. So far, as reported by State/UTs, as on 20.03.2025 out of 19.36 Crore
rural households in the country, approximately 15.53 Crore (80.22%) households
have been provided with safe drinking water through tap water supply.
With respect to Urban areas, Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban
Transformation (AMRUT) 2.0 scheme has been launched on 01 October 2021 in
all Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)/ cities, with an aim to enable them to become 'self-
reliant' and 'water secure'. Supplying clean water is also covered under the scope
of AMRUT 2.0 and so far, 3,587 water supply projects have been approved
across the country, covering various cities.
(c): Analysis of available data indicates that there has been overall improvement
in ground water situation in the country over the past few years, except in some
water stressed pockets. During the period from 2017 to 2024, annual ground
03.04.2025 932
water recharge in the country has increased from 436.15 Billion Cubic Meters
(BCM) to 446.90 BCM, whereas, total annual ground water extraction for all
purposes has remained stable from 244.92 BCM to 245.64 BCM. Further, the
Stage of Ground water Extraction (SoE), which is defined as a ratio of total annual
ground water extraction to total annual extractable ground water, has declined
from 61.6% to 60.47%, thus indicating reduction in ground water stress.
Further, arresting the decline of ground water is an important outcome
indicator under Atal Bhujal Yojana. As per the recent assessment conducted,
1,333 Atal Jal Gram Panchayats and 61 blocks in the year 2024 have shown
improvement in groundwater levels.
(d): Source sustainability is an important focus area of the schemes and projects
undertaken by the Ministry of Jal Shakti. Schemes like Groundwater Management
and Regulation Scheme, Atal Bhujal Yojana, Jal Shakti Abhiyan, etc. strive to
ensure sustainability of ground water sources, especially those which are being
utilized for drinking purposes. Measures like construction of rain water harvesting
and artificial recharge structures, which include ponds, check dams, recharge
shafts, rooftop rain water harvesting structures etc. are taken up under these
schemes for augmenting aquifer potential and to ensure their sustainability.
Further, development of reliable drinking water sources and/ or
augmentation of existing sources to provide long-term sustainability of water
supply system in villages is an integral part of Jal Jeevan Mission. To achieve this
03.04.2025 933
objective, the operational guidelines for the implementation of JJM stipulate that
any water supply scheme should be taken up under JJM only after the
recommendation of a Source Finding Committee of the respective state
government. This ensures that the identified water source through which the
scheme is planned, has sufficient yield for sustaining water supply as per required
norm, for the scheme design period. Additionally, strengthening of drinking water
sources is also taken up in convergence with other schemes such as MGNREGS,
Finance Commission grants to rural local bodies/ PRIs, MP and MLA’s Local
Area Development Fund, District Mineral Development Fund, CSR fund, etc.
(e) :Water is a state subject and regulation of ground water extraction is primarily
the responsibility of State governments. However, M/o Jal Shakti has constituted
Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) which regulates the abstraction cum use
of groundwater as per the provisions of its Guidelines dated 24.09.2020 which
have pan India applicability. The Guidelines contain sufficiently stringent
measures like imposition of Environmental Compensation (EC) charges for
extraction of ground water without valid NOC, penalties for non-compliance with
NOC conditions, sealing of unauthorized ground water extraction structures etc.
In addition to this, the state governments have been given the liberty to impose
more stringent measures in their domain, wherever felt necessary.

NH PROJECTS IN CHITRADURG
5451. SHRI GOVIND MAKTHAPPA KARJOL:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the ongoing and pending National Highways (NHs) projects in
Chitradurga Parliamentary Constituency, project-wise;
03.04.2025 941
(b) the details of the funds allocated, sanctioned and utilised in the said projects;
and
(c) the details of the steps taken by the Government to complete these national
projects at the earliest?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) and (b): The details of the ongoing National Highway (NH) projects for
Chitradurga Parliamentary Constituency in the State of Karnataka along with their
sanctioned cost and expenditure incurred are enclosed as Statement. As regards
allocation of funds for these projects, project- wise funds are not allocated and
funds are released out of overall allocation for the State during a financial year.
(c): To fast-track these projects, various steps such as streamlining of Land
Acquisition and Clearances, close coordination with all stakeholders, revamping
of dispute resolution mechanism, frequent review meetings with project
developers, State Governments and contractors at various levels etc. have been
taken up.
