SLUM REHABILITATION PROJECTS IN MUMBAI
5452. SHRI SANJAY DINA PATIL:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the reasons for continuous delays in granting the approvals for slum
rehabilitation projects on the Union Government-owned land in Mumbai,
specifically CTS No. 73 in Ghatkopar (West) which has deprived the residents of
the benefits under the Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) scheme;
03.04.2025 944
(b) whether a large slum settlement on this land has been awaiting redevelopment
under the SRA scheme of the State Government of Maharashtra for nearly thirty
years and if so, the reasons for the delay despite multiple efforts by local
representatives including former MPs and MLAs and if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the ownership of this land by the Union Government has been a
barrier to its inclusion in the SRA scheme and if so, the details thereof along with
the steps taken/being taken to address this issue and facilitate its inclusion;
(d) whether the Union Government has previously facilitated slum redevelopment
projects in other States and if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government would take corrective steps to approve the inclusion
of CTS No. 73 under the SRA scheme to ensure the welfare of the affected
families and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (e): ‘Land’ and ‘Colonisation’ are State subjects. Schemes related to
housing and slum rehabilitation along with its monitoring and supervision are
implemented by State/Union Territory (UT) Governments in their respective
regions. Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) is State agency, implementing slum
rehabilitation scheme according to its own regulation and bylaws.
03.04.2025 945
However, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) supplements the
efforts of States/UTs by providing Central Assistance under Pradhan Mantri Awas
Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U) to provide pucca house with basic amenities to eligible
urban beneficiaries across the country including slum dwellers. Eligible
beneficiaries can avail benefit of PMAY-U through available four verticals i.e.,
Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), In-
Situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR) and Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS).
The scheme provided flexibility to the beneficiaries including slum dwellers to
avail the benefit under any vertical as per the requirement and eligibility criteria.
ISSR vertical of PMAY-U is specifically for redevelopment of slum using land as a
resource for providing houses to eligible slum dwellers. However, slum dwellers
could take benefit under any one of the verticals.
As per scheme guidelines, Central Government land owning agencies
could also undertake “in-situ” slum redevelopment on their lands occupied by
slum dwellers under ISSR vertical of PMAY-U. Land owning agencies have their
own policies of land management and States/UTs may coordinate with such
agencies for relocation/redevelopment of slums on such land. Central Sanctioning
and Monitoring Committee (CSMC) under the Ministry considers Central
Assistance under PMAY-U, if the proposals are forwarded to the Ministry after the
approval of State Level Sanctioning and Monitoring Committee (SLSMC) headed
by Chief Secretray of States/UTs as per the scheme guidelines. No project
03.04.2025 946
proposal for Union Government – owned land in Mumbai, including CTS No.73
in Ghatkopar (West) has been received by the Ministry.
Further, based on the learning from the experiences of 9 years
implementation of PMAY-U, MoHUA has revamped the scheme and launched
PMAY-U 2.0 ‘Housing for All’ Mission with effect from 01.09.2024 for
implementation in urban areas across the country for one crore additional eligible
beneficiaries including slum dwellers through four verticals i.e., Beneficiary Led
Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), Affordable Rental
Housing (ARH) and Interest Subsidy Scheme (ISS). The eligible slum dwellers
may seek benefits of any vertical of PMAY-U 2.0. The scheme guidelines and
Unified Web-portal for submitting the online applications can be accessed through
https://pmay-urban.gov.in.
NEGATIVE EFFECTS OF BUILDING WATERWAYS USING CEMENT
CONCRETE
5453. DR. THOL THIRUMAAVALAVAN:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any data pertaining to the negative effects of
building waterways using cement concrete on the preservation of groundwater
levels;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
03.04.2025 947
(c) whether the Government has any plan to substitute permeable materials in
building waterways, water channels, canals, etc., to harvest and raise
groundwater levels; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) to (d) :Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) is responsible for regulation
and development of inland waterways {which have been declared as National
Waterways-(NWs) through the National Waterways Act, 2016} for shipping and
navigation as per IWAI Act, 1985. NWs are taken up for development as per the
outcome of first stage feasibility studies report and thereafter second stage
detailed project reports (DPRs). Waterways are not built artificially by IWAI. There
is no use of Cement concrete in maintaining navigable depths.
As per Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), assessment of Dynamic
Ground Water Resources of each State/UT is being carried out jointly by CGWB
and State Nodal/Ground Water Department annually since 2022 as per the
Ground Water Estimation Committee-2015 (GEC-2015) methodology. This
methodology includes norms for estimating groundwater recharge from canals,
taking into account whether the canal is lined or unlined.
03.04.2025 948
REDUCTION IN ALLOCATION OF BUDGET
5454. KUMARI SELJA:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the budgetary allocation for the Ministry of Civil Aviation has been
reduced to Rupees 2,357.14 crore for the financial year 2025-26 and if so, the
detail thereof;
(b) whether the Government has assessed the impact of these budgetary cuts on
critical areas such as airport infrastructure development and regional connectivity,
particularly under the UDAN scheme; and
(c) if so, the details thereof along with the steps being taken to ensure
uninterrupted expansion and development of the aviation sector despite the
reduced allocation?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) :The Budget Estimates 2024-25 is Rs 2357.14 crore against which the revised
allocation for the year 2024-25 has been increased to Rs 2658.68 crore with the
total increase of Rs 301.54 crore. The Budget Estimates 2025-26 is Rs 2400.31
crore.
03.04.2025 949
(b) and (c): The government has announced the launch of modified UDAN
Scheme to increase regional connectivity to 120 new destinations and cater to 4
crore passengers in the next 10 years. The proposal for budgetary allocation will
be submitted on formulation of the modified UDAN Scheme.
Ministry of Civil Aviation does not have any separate Budgetary
Allocation/Funds for the development of Greenfield Airports.
However, for development/upgradation/modernisation of various airports in
the country to meet the requirement of Air traffic /passengers' growth, Airports
Authority of India and PPP Partners have embarked upon a CAPEX plan of more
than Rs. 91000 crore during FY 2019-20 to FY 2024-25. Out of this, approximate
expenditure of Rs. 88000 crore has already been incurred under NIP till February,
2025.

ELEVATED EXPRESSWAY IN TAMIL NADU
5457. SHRI K. GOPINATH:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the details and current status of the approvals pending for the elevated
expressway between Tambaram and Chengalpattu in Tamil Nadu and the specific
challenges or bottlenecks causing delays in the approval process for this project;
03.04.2025 963
(b) the steps taken/being taken to expedite the approval process and the
expected timeline for obtaining all the necessary clearances;
(c) the details of financial and technical support provided/proposed to be provided
by the Union Government for the said project; and
(d) the details of other elevated expressway projects approved or pending during
the last ten years, State-wise and the details of financial and technical support
provided thereto?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) to (c):The Government takes up proposals of ring roads, bypasses and
elevated corridors for development in major cities/ urban centers and State
capitals in consultation with State Governments to de-congest traffic and alleviate
choke points on National Highways. As far as the de-congestion of the stretch
between Tambaram and Chengalpattu of NH-32 is concerned, the work for
preparing the Detailed Project Report (DPR), for the development of the stretch
from Chennai and Trichy is awarded including the option to explore the possibility
for construction of elevated road in the said stretch. Decision will be taken based
on outcome of DPR, traffic density, speed drop, number of road entering/exiting
the city, condition of roads, inter-se priority and synergy with PM Gati Shakti
National Master Plan.
03.04.2025 964
(d): DPR/ Feasibility is done for evaluating options for development of National
Highways. In certain section of NHs, in case of constraints in the acquisition of
land or very high volume of traffic, such sections of highway are either realigned/
elevated. Such decisions are based on the requirement of connectivity, projected
traffic, inter-se priority and synergy with PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
Further, in order to improve viability of the project, the State Government is at
times requested to agree to share land acquisition cost, utility shifting cost,
reimbursement of royalty and State component of GST. Such agreements are
project specific.
LAND ACQUISITION FOR THE GREENFIELD HIGHWAY
5458. SHRI VAMSI KRISHNA GADDAM:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the opposition from farmers in
Lakshettipet and Mancherial districts to the land acquisition for National Highway
(NH)-163;
(b) if so, the details thereof, including the total area of agricultural land proposed
to be acquired for the Greenfield Highway along with the number of affected
families and the compensation package proposed for them;
03.04.2025 965
(c) whether the Government has explored the feasibility of expanding the existing
NH-163 to four lanes (Brownfield expansion) instead of constructing a new
Greenfield Highway, thereby minimizing land acquisition and displacement;
(d) if so, the details of the feasibility studies conducted, including their findings;
and
(e) if not, the reasons for not considering a Brownfield expansion therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): Yes sir. Lakshettipett and Macherial are located on NH-63, and not on NH
163.
(b): The details of the total agricultural land proposed to be acquired for the
Greenfield alignment from Lakshettipett town to Mancherial Town along with the
number of affected families and the compensation package proposed are as
below:
Sl. Total agriculture land to be Total No. of Compensation package
No acquired in Ha. farmers affected proposed Rs. in cr.
1 112.00 1014 112
(c), (d) and (e): Yes sir. National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has explored
the feasibility of expanding the existing NH-63 alignment, but from Lakshettipett
03.04.2025 966
town to Mulkalla village thick ribbon development has taken place along existing
NH-63 road for about 15 km from km 136+000 up to km 151+100 involving
demolition of several residential houses and commercial establishments. Also
drinking water supply pipelines installed under Mission Bhagiratha is running on
both sides of the existing alingment, making it difficult for the widening of the
existing road. Requests have been received from public representatives for
construction of bypass to Lakshettipett town, instead of widening the existing
road.
Based on site conditions and keeping the representations into
consideration, the alignment away from the existing NH-63 road from km 125+000
(Rayapatnam Junction) to km 151+000 (Mulkalla Village) was proposed as a
bypass to Lakshettipett Town in Mancherial District.

जल कने(cid:179)शन थे। तब से, 1,24,535 (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) को जेजेएम के तहत नल जल कने(cid:179)शन (cid:255)दान
िकए गए ह(cid:167)। इस (cid:255)कार, 01.04.2025 तक, िजल े म(cid:164) 2,69,130 (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) म(cid:164) से 1,53,017
(56.86%) (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) के पास नल जल आपूित(cid:170) का (cid:255)ावधान उपल(cid:202)ध है।
राज(cid:214)थान सरकार (cid:300)ारा दी गई सूचना के अनुसार, रा(cid:186)य ने झंझु नु ू, च(cid:322)ु और सीकर िजल(cid:332) के (cid:250)ामीण

बनाई है।

03.04.2025 972
BALLOONING INTERNATIONAL EVENT
5461. SHRI RAJIV PRATAP RUDI:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether it is a fact that a Ballooning International Event is held in the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof including the locations and frequency of the event;
(c) whether the Government has provided any financial or other support to the
organizing committee for promoting the event internationally;
(d) if so, the details thereof including the amount of financial assistance, logistical
aid and policy incentives provided; and
(e) whether the Government has any plans to further promote ballooning as an
aero sport in the country through policy initiatives or international collaborations
and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) and (b): The Plenary Meeting of the Ballooning Commission (CIA) of the
Federation Aeronautique Internationale (FAI), the World Air Sports Body, was
hosted in India by Aero Club of India- National Airsport Control from 12th to 15th
March 2025 at New Delhi. This is an annual event and is traditionally hosted by a
03.04.2025 973
member country or the FAI Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland. This was
held in India for the first time. This milestone event significantly bolstered India's
prominence within the international ballooning community, while elevating its
reputation in hosting prestigious international forums.
(c) and (d):The Ministry has sanctioned a grant of Rs.35 lakhs for hosting of the
Plenary Meeting of the Ballooning from 12th to 15th March 2025.
(e) :The major step taken by Government to make India one of the top Air Sports
nations is the promulgation of National Air Sports Guidelines (NASG) in August
2023 by Ministry of Civil Aviation (MoCA).The main objectives of NASG published
by Ministry of Civil Aviation include the following:
i) Promote an air sports culture in the country including ballooning.
ii) Enable adoption of international standard and good practices in safety air
sports infrastructure, equipment, operations and maintenance and training
iii) Develop a simple, stakeholder-friendly transparent, accountable and effective
governance structure
iv) Enhance participation and success of Indian sportspersons in global air sports
events; and
v) Promote design, development and manufacturing of air sports equipment in
India in line with the Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan.
03.04.2025 974
IMPLEMENTATION OF CITIIS SCHEME
5462. SHRI APPALANAIDU KALISETTI:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the projects approved under City Investments to Innovate,
Integrate and Sustain (CITIIS) 1.0 and CITIIS 2.0, city and State-wise;
(b) the details of the total funds allocated, sanctioned and utilized under both
phases of the scheme along with a breakdown of loans and grants from
international partners;
(c) the current status of the implementation of each project under CITIIS 1.0 and
2.0 including expected completion timelines and delays, if any;
(d) the number and details of proposals pending for approval along with the
reasons for the delay and the expected timeline for clearance;
(e) the criteria used for selection of cities and projects under CITIIS 2.0;
(f) whether any new cities would be included in the future and if so, the details
thereof; and
(g) whether the Government proposes to expand the scope of CITIIS beyond
smart cities and provide additional funding support to smaller urban areas and if
so, the details thereof?
03.04.2025 975
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a): 12 cities were selected under the City Investments to Innovate, Integrate and
Sustain (CITIIS) 1.0 program across the country.
CITIIS 2.0 program was launched on 31.05.2023 for envisaging comprehensive
support to the competitively selected projects promoting circular economy with
focus on integrated waste management at the city level, climate-oriented reform
actions at the State level, and institutional strengthening and knowledge
dissemination at the National level.
Under CITIIS 2.0, 18 cities under Component 1 (at city-level action) and 21
States under component-2 (at State level action) were selected.
Details of the projects approved under CITIIS 1.0 and CITIIS 2.0 under
component-1 and component-2 are at Statement-I, Statement-II and Statement-
III.
(b) and (c): Under CITIIS 1.0, ₹ 771.99 crore have been allocated, out of which,
₹753.48 crore have been released to the cities and ₹662.88 crore have been
utilized so far. Under CITIIS 1.0, the projects in Amritsar, Bhubaneswar,
Dehradun, Kochi, Surat and Visakhapatnam have been completed. The projects
in Agartala, Amaravati, Chennai, Hubballi-Dharwad, Puducherry and Ujjain are
ongoing.
03.04.2025 976
Under the CITIIS 2.0 program, the project agreements with cities and
States have been signed on 03rd March 2025. Under CITIIS 2.0, ₹1920 crore
have been allocated, out of which ₹84.5 crore have been released, detail of which
are at Statement-IV.
(d) and (e): All 100 Cities selected under the Smart Cities Mission were eligible to
apply for the CITIIS 2.0 challenge. 84 cities submitted their proposals, out of
which 18 cities were selected through the criteria stipulated in the CITIIS
Operational Guidelines.
(f) : No.
(g) : Government of India has approved the CITIIS 2.0 up to 31.12.2027.

DIFFICULTIES FACED BY MSMEs
5464. SHRIMATI KANIMOZHI KARUNANIDHI:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the issues faced by micro, small and
medium enterprises due to the 45 days payment clause introduced through
Section 43B(h) of the Income Tax Act and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government is planning to remove or relax the provisions of the
clause considering the demand of the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
(MSMEs) associations for enabling a better business environment for the MSMEs;
and
(c) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
03.04.2025 984
(a) to (c): To ensure timely payments to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
(MSMEs) vide Finance Act 2023, clause (h) was inserted in Section 43B of the
Income-tax Act 1961. The clause provides that any sum payable by the assessee
to a Micro and Small Enterprise (MSE) beyond the time limit specified in section
15 of the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development Act, 2006, which
cannot be more than 45 days, shall be allowed as a deduction only on actual
payment.
A number of stakeholders, including MSME associations have endorsed
the intervention introduced to ensure timely payments as being beneficial to the
Micro and Small Enterprises, which were grappling with the issue of delayed
payments, cash flow and the risk of business getting closed due to financial
constraints.
MOVEMENT OF OVERSIZED CONSIGNMENTS ON NHS
5465. SHRI RAJABHAU PARAG PRAKASH WAJE:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has acknowledged the increase in mechanized
farming and the need for larger farming machines such as harvesters to access
all fertile lands;
03.04.2025 985
(b) if so, whether structures like Light Vehicular Underpasses (LVUPs), Vehicular
Overpasses (VOPs) and Pedestrian Underpasses (PUPs) on expressways and
National Highways (NHs) are restricting their movement, if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government has taken note of the growing demand for oversized
consignments due to industrial expansion;
(d) if so, whether the proposed VUPs, LVUPs, VOPs, Road Over Bridges (ROBs)
and grade-separated junctions are limiting their transportation and hindering
development, if so, the details thereof;
(e) whether the Government recognizes that the lack of service roads in rural or
less populated areas along expressways is restricting local movement and
development, if so, the details thereof; and
(f) whether the Government has conducted any study on the economic impact of
limitations in oversized consignment movement and large cultivation equipment,
including increased transportation costs, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): Yes, sir.
(b):National Highways and Expressways are designed and constructed to carry
traffic carrying loads in vehicles of specified dimensions as per norms for efficient
and economical transport of goods and passengers. However, keeping in view the
developmental needs of the country, movement of Overweight/Over Dimensional
Consignments (OWCs/ODCs) carrying industrial goods on National Highways
03.04.2025 986
(NHs)/Expressways is allowed in accordance with established policy. National
Highways and Expressways also have provisions of vehicular under/overpass at
the intersection of National Highways, State Highways, Major District Roads and
across other categories of roads as per site requirements to maintain continuity
for local traffic and facilitate the movement of agricultural machinery. Vertical
clearances of Light Vehicular Underpasses (LVUPs), Vehicular Overpasses
(VoPs) and Pedestrian Underpasses (PUPs) are provided as per the applicable
Indian Roads Congress codal provisions and the extant policies of the
government as well as site requirements to have un-restricted movement of larger
farming machines such as harvesters to access all fertile lands.
(c) , (d) and (f): The Government has established mechanism for grant of online
permission for movement of Overweight/Over Dimensional Consignments
(OWCs/ODCs) carrying industrial goods on National Highways (NHs) based on
analytical study and also issued policy for design of structures such as Bridges,
VUPs, LVUPs, VOPs, Road Over Bridges (ROBs) and grade-separated junctions
with special loading catering for uninterrupted movement of OWCs/ODCs as well
as prevention of damage to any associated structures.
(e): Expressways are designed in conformity with Indian Roads Congress
Guidelines IRC: SP: 99-2023 ‘Manual of Specifications and Standards for
Expressways’. It has the provision of access path for all along the expressways
for local movement.
03.04.2025 987

नोजल िब(cid:248)(cid:236) को (cid:214)वचािलत (cid:322)प से रोक देता है आिद।
सेवा उ(cid:198)मुख पहल: लॉयािलटी और (cid:229)रवाड(cid:170)स काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म िजसम(cid:164) छूट, कै शबैक और (cid:229)रवाड(cid:170)
(cid:200) वाईटं , सात(cid:332) िदन 24 घंटे (cid:250)ाहक सहायता, आरओ सुिवधाओ ं और सेवाओ ं क(cid:236) त(cid:194)काल रेिटंग क(cid:236)

PRADHAN MANTRI JANJATIYA VIKAS MISSION IN MAHARASHTRA
5468. ADV GOWAAL KAGADA PADAVI:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has achieved its goals under the Pradhan Mantri
Janjatiya Vikas Mission (PMJVM) to promote livelihood opportunities for tribals in
Maharashtra;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) the steps being taken by the Government to promote Mahua seeds and other
Minor Forest Produce from the tribal districts of Maharashtra; and
(d) whether the Government has taken any initiatives for promoting and
facilitating the livelihood of the tribals of Nandurbar Parliamentary Constituency
through PMJVM and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a) and (b): Under the scheme,“Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission
(PMJVM)”, the Ministry of Tribal Affairs provides funds for setting up Van Dhan
Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) which are primarily clusters of tribal SHGs formed to
reap economies of scale through value addition and marketing of MFPs / non-
MFPs. As on 28.02.2025, an amount of Rs. 3,975 lakhs has been sanctioned for
establishment of 265 VDVKs associating about 79,500 beneficiaries in the State
of Maharashtra.
(c) :Under the PMJVM scheme, the Ministry also provides revolving funds to
State Governments for procurement of Minor Forest Produces (MFPs), including
03.04.2025 1000
Mahua seeds, at Minimum Support Price (MSP). The list of MFPs for which MSP
is provided is given at https://trifed.tribal.gov.in/sites/default/files/2022-
02/List%20of%2087%20MFPs.pdf.
(d): So far, an amount of Rs. 90.00 lakhs has been sanctioned for establishment
of 6 VDVKs in Nandurbar District, Maharashtra associating 1800 beneficiaries.
Besides, under PMJVM, Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development Federation
(TRIFED) also undertakes empanelment of tribal artisans, procurement of
various tribal products from them for generating livelihood opportunities for tribal
communities and undertakes retail marketing of tribal products through TRIBES
India Outlets, E-Commerce platforms and Exhibitions like Aadi Mahotsav, Aadi
Bazaar etc. So far, 2 tribal suppliers/ producers comprising of SHG and VDVK
from Nandurbar of Maharashtra are empanelled with TRIFED.
HIKE IN AIRFARES
5469. DR. T. SUMATHY ALIAS THAMIZHACHI THANGAPANDIAN:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether it is true that the Government has no control over the airfares fixed
by airlines and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government proposes to constitute a high-level committee to
look into the issues of dynamic pricing leading to exorbitant air ticket prices on
03.04.2025 1001
certain domestic and international routes, if so, the details thereof and if not, the
reasons therefor;
(c) whether the airline ticket prices are determined by the demand and supply
theory and if so, the details thereof; and
(d) the proactive steps taken by the Government to improve demand and supply
by increasing the number of airlines and seats available on such routes where
the demand is very high?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (d): Airlines determine their airfares based on their operational needs,
while adhering to Rule 135 of the Aircraft Rules, 1937. Airfares are not subject to
regulation by the Government.;
Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has setup a Tariff Monitoring Unit
(TMU) that monitors airfares on select domestic sectors on random basis by
using airlines' websites data on monthly basis to ensure that the airlines do not
charge airfares outside the range declared by them.;
Enhancement of capacity through the induction of additional aircraft fleets,
the modernization of existing airports, and the development of new airports have
rendered air travel more accessible to the general public. This expansion has
consequently led to a significant increase in domestic air traffic, as evidenced by
03.04.2025 1002
the rise in passenger volumes from 70 million in the fiscal year 2014-15 to 153
million in the fiscal year 2023-24.This upward trend continues in 2024-25.
Further, the Indian airlines have placed an order for around 1150 new aircraft in
last 02 years.;
With continuous engagement with airlines and Online Ticketing Agents
(OTAs) and keeping a watch on the movement of airfares, the airfares have
moderated in 2024 relative to 2023 on sectors monitored by DGCA.;
To maintain market competitiveness, the Government remains vigilant and
intervenes to shift capacity from one sector to another to prevent exorbitant
pricing to ensure passenger comfort and welfare.;
The airfares are dynamic in nature and follow the principle of demand and
supply. The trends in airfare prices in India exhibit considerable seasonality,
prevailing fuel price, the capacity of the aircraft operating on the route,
competition on the sector, season, holidays, festivals, long weekends, events
(sports, fairs, contests) etc.;
जनजातीय लोग(cid:332) का िवकास

िकतनी िनिध आवंिटत क(cid:236) गई है?
जनजातीय काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी दुगा(cid:170) दास उइके ):

PROJECTS SANCTIONED UNDER PMKSY IN KARNATAKA
5471. SHRI KOTA SRINIVASA POOJARY:
03.04.2025 1004
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the number of projects sanctioned and made functional under
the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana (PMKSY) in Karnataka, particularly
in the districts of Dakshina Kannada, Udupi, Chikmagalur, Hassan and Uttara
Kannada since its launch; and
(b) the details of the total number of direct and indirect employment opportunities
generated from such projects in the said districts?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) and (b): Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) is implementing a
centrally sponsored umbrella scheme Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana
(PMKSY). Under component schemes of PMKSY, Ministry provides financial
assistance for setting up food processing projects and related infrastructure
across the country, including Karnataka.
These schemes are not any region or State/UT specific but are demand driven
and implemented across the country. Applications / proposals are invited from
eligible entities as per the extant scheme guidelines of the relevant sub-scheme
under PMKSY through floating of Expression of Interest from time to time based
on availability of funds. The proposals received are scrutinized for their eligibility
and evaluated based on the criteria fixed as per the extant scheme guidelines.
03.04.2025 1005
Merit based approvals are given to the eligible proposals based on availability of
funds.
Under component schemes of PMKSY, 96 projects are approved in the
State of Karnataka and 10 projects in the districts of Dakshina Kannada, Udupi,
Chikmagalur, Hassan and Uttara Kannada since launch of component schemes
of PMKSY and it is estimated that these projects generated about 4535 number
of direct/indirect employment. The details of these 10 projects approved in
Dakshina Kannada, Udupi, Chikmagalur, Hassan and Uttara Kannada are at
Statement.

(क) स े (ग) : भारत सरकार (जीओआई) ने वष(cid:170) 2014 म(cid:164) दीनदयाल उपा(cid:197)याय (cid:250)ाम (cid:186)योित योजना
(डीडीयूजीजेवाई) श(cid:322)ु क(cid:236) थी। रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा दी गई जानकारी के अनसु ार, दशे के सभी आबाद गैर-
िव(cid:299)तु ीकृत जनगणना गांव(cid:332) को िदनांक 28 अ(cid:255)ैल, 2018 तक िव(cid:299)तु ीकृत कर िदया गया।
डीडीयूजीजेवाई के तहत उ(cid:176)राखंड रा(cid:186)य के 91 गांव(cid:332) सिहत रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय (cid:214)तर पर कुल 18,374 गांव(cid:332)
को िव(cid:299)तु ीकृत िकया गया। इसके अलावा, (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी सहज िबजली हर घर योजना (सौभा(cid:181)य) के
तहत (cid:250)ामीण (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) सभी इ(cid:184)छुक घर(cid:332) और शहरी (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) सभी इ(cid:184)छुक गरीब घर(cid:332) को िव(cid:299)तु ीकृत
िकया गया, िजनम(cid:164) से उ(cid:176)राखंड रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) 2,48,751 घर(cid:332) को िव(cid:299)तु ीकृत िकया गया।
डीडीयूजीजेवाई का (cid:255)भाव आकलन अ(cid:197)ययन मेसस(cid:170) अ(cid:198)(cid:214)ट(cid:170) एडं यंग एलएलपी (cid:300)ारा वष(cid:170)
2022 म (cid:164) अिखल भारतीय (cid:214)तर पर िकया गया था, िजसम(cid:164) बताया गया था िक अ(cid:197)ययन के अंतग(cid:170)त
शािमल 100% गांव(cid:332) और उपभो(cid:265)ाओ ंने िव(cid:299)तु आपूित(cid:170) के घंट(cid:332) म(cid:164) सुधार क(cid:236) सूचना दी है। अ(cid:197)ययन
म(cid:164) उ(cid:208)लेख िकया गया है िक कई िड(cid:214)कॉम ने नए अवसंरचना के कारण िवतरण हािनय(cid:332) को कम िकया
है। िबिलंग द(cid:177)ता और एसीएस-एआरआर अंतर म (cid:164) सधु ार, िड(cid:214)कॉम के (cid:255)चालन और िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:255)दश(cid:170)न
के मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170) संके तक, ने भी डीडीयूजीजेवाई के बाद सधु ार िदखाई िदया है। तथािप, यह उ(cid:208)लेख
करना उिचत है िक, जैसा िक (cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) म(cid:164) बताया गया है, डीडीयूजीजेवाई के काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन के दौरान,
िड(cid:214)कॉम ने अ(cid:198)य (cid:214)क(cid:236)म(cid:332) के तहत आपूित(cid:170) (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म (cid:164) िवकास काय(cid:170) िकए, िजसका िविभ(cid:198)न मापदडं (cid:332) म (cid:164)
सधु ार म(cid:164) योगदान हो सकता है।

PROJECTS SANCTIONED UNDER RDSS
5474. SHRI B. K. PARTHASARATHI:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the total number of projects sanctioned under the Revamped
Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS), State and year-wise till date;
(b) the details of the funds sanctioned and released for the approved projects,
State and year wise;
(c) whether the Government has any details regarding the total number of smart
meters sanctioned under the said scheme;
(d) if so, the details thereof, State-wise and district-wise for Andhra Pradesh
along with the sanctioned and awarded quantities of smart meters; and
03.04.2025 1014
(e) the details of the cost of the sanctioned project, grants allocated and
disbursed for smart meters in the State of Andhra Pradesh?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) to (d) : Government of India launched the Revamped Distribution Sector
Scheme (RDSS) in July 2021 with the objective of improving the quality and
reliability of power supply to consumers through a financially sustainable and
operationally efficient distribution Sector. Under the scheme, projects worth Rs.
1.51 lakh crore for loss reduction infrastructure and Rs. 1.31 lakh crore for smart
metering works have been sanctioned. The State/ UT-wise and year-wise details
of loss-reduction and smart metering works are placed at Statement-IA, IB, and
IC.
As part of the scheme, Government of India has been supporting grid-based
electrification of households left out during SAUBHAGYA (Pradhan Mantri Sahaj
Bijli Har Ghar Yojana) period, wherever found feasible. Till date, works
amounting to Rs. 6,113 Cr. have been sanctioned for electrification of 12,91,833
households. This includes electrification works for households belonging to
Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) identified under PM-JANMAN
(Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan), households belonging to
Scheduled Tribes identified under DA-JGUA (Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram
Utkarsh Abhiyan) and households identified under PM-AJAY (Pradhan Mantri
03.04.2025 1015
Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday Yojana). The State/UT-wise and year-wise details of
household electrification works sanctioned under RDSS are placed at
Statement-lIA and IIB.
The State/UT-wise and year-wise details of funds expended under RDSS
for loss reduction works are at Statement-III.
State-wise details of smart-meter works sanctioned are placed at
Statement-IV. District-wise details of smart metering works sanctioned and
awarded under RDSS for State of Andhra Pradesh are at Statement-V.
(e) : DISCOM-wise details of sanctioned cost, grants allocated and disbursed for
smart meters under RDSS are as under:
Project Cost GoI Grant GoI grant
expended

CHALLENGES IN STRENGTHENING THE FOOD PROCESSING SECTOR IN
HINGOLI
5475. SHRI NAGESH BAPURAO AASHTIKAR PATIL:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has conducted any study or survey to assess the
gaps and challenges in the food processing sector in Hingoli and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to set up food parks,
processing hubs and value-addition centres in Hingoli to boost local food
processing industries;
03.04.2025 1030
(c) whether there is any ongoing or proposed cold storage and warehouse
projects in Hingoli to reduce post-harvest losses and ensure better storage for
perishable agricultural produce, if so, the details thereof;
(d) the details of financial assistance and subsidy programmes available for new
entrepreneurs and small -scale food processing industries in Hingoli and the
manner in which the Government is ensuring awareness about these schemes;
and
(e) the status of export promotion initiatives for processed food products from
Hingoli along with the efforts made by the Government to connect local
businesses with national and international markets?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a): Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has not conducted any study
to assess the gaps and challenges in the food processing sector in the country
including Hingoli.
(b) to (d): For overall development of food processing sector, MoFPI implements
Central Sector umbrella scheme – Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana
(PMKSY) since 2016-17 across the country including the Hingoli district of
Maharashtra. The components scheme under PMKSY are (i) Mega Food Parks
(MFP scheme- discontinued w.e.f. 01.04.2021), (ii) Integrated Cold Chain and
Value Addition Infrastructure (Cold Chain scheme), (iii) Creation of Infrastructure
for Agro Processing Clusters (APC scheme), (iv) Creation/ Expansion of Food
03.04.2025 1031
Processing and Preservation Capacities (CEFPPC scheme), (v) Creation of
Backward and Forward Linkages (CBFL scheme- discontinued w.e.f.
01.04.2021) and (vi) Operation Greens (OG scheme). The schemes are demand
driven and proposals are invited by floating Expression of Interest from time to
time based on availability of funds. Under these component schemes, MoFPI
provides financial support in the form of grants-in-aid/ subsidy for setting up of
food processing projects thereby creating both processing and preservation
infrastructure facilities including cold chain infrastructure. The facilities created
under the component schemes help in preservation and processing of raw farm
produce and transport of raw and finished products thereby reducing post-
harvest losses of farm produce. As on 28th February, 2025, one project under
CEFPPC scheme in Hingoli is under implementation with project cost of
Rs.19.96 crore.
MoFPI provides financial, technical and business support for setting up/
upgradation of micro food processing enterprises in the country under "Pradhan
Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme.
The support is provided for potential entrepreneurs for all the products including
One District One Product (ODOP) products under PMFME Scheme across the
country including Hingoli district. For the district of Hingoli, 184 loans have been
sanctioned with approved subsidy of Rs. 12.05 crore under the scheme. MoFPI
is taking various initiatives to promote and increase awareness and participation
among beneficiaries through National and State level awareness campaigns
03.04.2025 1032
such as Newspaper advertisement, Radio Jingles, Exhibitions and Expos, Millet
Fairs, Buyer-Seller Meets etc. since the inception of the scheme.
(e): Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industries
(PLISFPI) of MoFPI is intended, inter alia, to support creation of global food
manufacturing champions and support Indian brands of food products in
international market. Also, Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export
Development Authority (APEDA) provides incentives to processed food
exporters under its different schemes for Infrastructure, Quality, Market
Development etc. These schemes are implemented across the country,
including Hingoli.
Apart from this, with the objective to boost investments and sourcing of
Indian food products, MoFPI organized 3rd edition of a Mega Event, called
“World Food India” during 19th to 22nd September 2024 at Bharat Mandapam,
New Delhi to showcase domestic Industry and provide it with collaborative
opportunities with international stakeholders. The event invited global food
processing companies, innovators, supply chain stakeholders, equipment
manufacturers etc. on a collaborative platform and to provide foreign companies
with tie-up/ business opportunities with its Indian counterparts
SHGS UNDER DAY-NULM
5476. SHRI DAGGUMALLA PRASADA RAO:
03.04.2025 1033
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the total number of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) created and loans disbursed
under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Urban Livelihoods Mission
(DAY-NULM) till date, State-wise, especially for Andhra Pradesh;
(b) the total number of candidates skill trained and placed under the said
scheme during the last five years and the current year, year and district-wise;
(c) the details of the types of skills developed by the beneficiaries, sector-wise;
(d) the details of the Central and State share allocated and released under the
said scheme till date, State-wise, especially for Andhra Pradesh; and
(e) whether the Government has received any proposals or plans to extend the
said scheme and if so, the details thereof including the timeline of its extension?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a): A statement showing the State/UT-wise number of Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
formed and loans disbursed under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National
Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM) from 01.04.2014 to 30.09.2024 is at
Statement-I.
(b): A statement showing the year-wise number of candidates skill trained and
placed under DAY-NULM during the last five years and the current year [i.e.
2019-20 to 2024-25 (upto 30.09.2024)] is at Statement-II.
(c):The Employment through Skill Training and Placement (ESTand P)
03.04.2025 1034
component under the Mission aimed to provide skill training to urban poor in
various market oriented courses including apparel, beauty culture and hair
dressing, construction, electrical, fashion design, food processing, hospitality, IT
and ITeS, Medical and Nursing etc.
(d):Under DAY-NULM financing was shared between the Centre and States in
the ratio 90:10 for North Eastern, Himalayan States and UT of Jammu and
Kashmir; 100% for all other UTs and 60:40 for remaining States. A statement
showing State/UT-wise central funds released under DAY-NULM from
01.04.2014 to 30.09.2024 including Andhra Pradesh is at Statement-III.
(e):The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) implemented DAY-
NULM upto 30.09.2024. Further, with effect from 1st October, 2024, MoHUA
launched a pilot on urban poverty alleviation in 25 select cities for an initial
period of 3 months, later on extended till 31st March, 2025

STATUS OF NULM
5477. SHRI SUDHAKAR SINGH:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:-
(a) the current status of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM) along
with the number of urban poor benefited from its various schemes since its
inception;
(b) the details of the funds allocated, released and utilized under NULM for skill
development, self-employment and urban poverty alleviation, State-wise;
(c) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure effective
implementation of NULM, particularly in economically weaker States like Bihar,
Jharkhand and Odisha;
(d) whether the Government has identified any gaps or challenges in the
implementation of NULM and if so, the details thereof along with the corrective
measures taken/being taken to improve the efficiency and reach for poor thereof;
and
(e) the measures taken/being taken by the Government to enhance employment
opportunities, promote Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and provide better financial
support for microenterprises under NULM?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a): The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, MoHUA implemented Deendayal
Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM) in 2014
03.04.2025 1042
and it ended on 30th September, 2024. The progress of DAY-NULM since its
inception until 30th September, 2024 is at Statement-I.
(b): Under DAY-NULM, Central Assistance is released to States/ UTs as a
whole. The inter-se allocation of funds under different components of the
Mission is done by the respective States/ Uts, as per their requirements. The
details of funds released and utilized are at Statement-II.
(c) and (d): The States/Uts effectively implemented the scheme DAY-NULM
through their respective Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) comprising various
components viz. Self-Employment Programme (SEP), Social Mobilization and
Institutional Development (SMID) etc. The Central Government from time to time
mentioned the implementation of the scheme through the following measures:
 Providing help to SHGs for selling their products through various online
and offline marketing strategies.
 Organising SHG (Self-Help Group) credit linkage melas with banks.
 Holding meetings with the State Level Bankers’ Committee (SLBC),
District Level Bankers’ Committee (DLBC), and Block Level Bankers’ Committee
(BLBC).
 Awareness campaigns have been conducted through various IEC
activities such as hoardings, banners, pamphlets and electronic and print
media.
 Review progress of mission with State Level Officers.
03.04.2025 1043
Based on the learnings of DAY-NULM, the pilot was launched with effect
from 1st October 2024 initially in 25 select cities and later expanded across the
country. The pilot extended till 31st March 2025.
(e): Under the Mission, a one-time Revolving Fund support of Rs. 10,000/- was
provided to SHGs, which were functional for a period of 3 months and had at
least 70% of members as urban poor. Mission offered interest subvention at the
rate over and above the rate of interest of 7% on the bank linkage loans
sanctioned to SHGs. An additional 3% interest subvention was provided to
women SHGs on timely payment of loans.

ROAD INFRASTRUCTURE IN TELANGANA
5479. SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the State Government of Telangana is planning to develop ten new
greenfield radial roads alongside metro corridor connecting outer ring road to
proposed Regional Ring Road (RRR) and has requested the Ministry to sanction
Rs. 45,000 crores as financial assistance; and
(b) if so, the details thereof along with the action taken thereon till date and the
funds sanctioned and spent therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) and (b): The Government in Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
(MoRTH) is primarily responsible for development and maintenance of National
Highways (NHs). Development and maintenance of NHs is a continuous
process. Works on NHs, including development of greenfield sections, are
03.04.2025 1049
accordingly taken up based on the traffic density, condition of the road, inter-se
priority and synergy with PM GatiShakti National Master Plan (NMP).
At present, 52 NHs projects in 1,423 km length costing ₹25,323 crore are
under construction in the State of Telangana. The Government has incurred
expenditure of ₹25,000 crore on development and maintenance of NHs in the
State of Telangana since April, 2019.
Government has also taken up preparation of Detailed Project Report for
Northern Regional Ring Road of Hyderabad. No proposal for development of
greenfield radial roads connecting outer ring road to Northern Regional Ring
Road is under consideration of Government.
Apart from above, the Government allocates and releases funds for State
Governments / Union Territories (UTs), including for the State of Telangana, for
development and maintenance of State Roads under the Central Road and
Infrastructure Fund (CRIF) Scheme as per the provisions of the amended CRIF
Act, 2000. Accordingly, the Government has released ₹1,722 crore to the State
of Telangana for development and maintenance of State Roads under CRIF
Scheme since April, 2019.
रा(cid:186)य के राजमाग(cid:334) का दोहरीकरण
5480. (cid:174)ी भाऊसाहबे राजाराम वाकचौरे:

संसदीय िनवा(cid:170)चन (cid:177)े(cid:253) के अंतग(cid:170)त रा(cid:186)य राजमाग(cid:170) (एसएच) सं(cid:180)या 23 (बोटा से राजरु ), एसएच-54,

कोई कदम उठाए ह(cid:167) या उठाने का िवचार है और यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ख) (cid:179)या सरकार को इस संबंध म(cid:164) जन-(cid:255)ितिनिधय(cid:332) से कोई अनरु ोध (cid:255)ा(cid:302) ह(cid:242)आ है; और
(ग) यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है तथा इस संबंध म(cid:164) अब तक (cid:179)या (cid:255)गित ह(cid:242)ई ह?ै
सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी िनितन जयराम गडकरी):
(क) से (ग): सरकार का सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:180)ु य (cid:322)प से रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय राजमाग(cid:334)
(एनएच) के िवकास और अनुर(cid:177)ण के िलए उ(cid:176)रदायी है। सरकार संबंिधत रा(cid:186)य/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)
(यूटी) क(cid:236) सरकार(cid:332) क(cid:236) िसफा(cid:229)रश(cid:332) के आधार पर क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय सड़क और अवसंरचना िनिध
(सीआरआईएफ) योजना के तहत रा(cid:186)य क(cid:236) सड़क(cid:332) के िवकास के िलए काय(cid:334) को भी मंजूरी देती है।

स(cid:180)ं या 23 (बोटा से राजूर), एसएच-54, एसएच-65, एसएच-50 और एसएच-320 के दोहरीकरण के

काय(cid:334) को मंजूरी दी गई है।
TRIFED INITIATIVES IN ODISHA
5481. SHRI SUKANTA KUMAR PANIGRAHI:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the projects and initiatives undertaken by the Tribal Cooperative
Marketing Development Federation of India (TRIFED) during the last three years
03.04.2025 1051
to support tribal artisans in Kandhamal, Odisha and promote the marketing of
tribal handicrafts, handlooms and forest produce at national and international
levels;
(b) the extent to which Kandhamal’s tribal communities have been benefited
from the Van Dhan Yojana and other TRIFED programmes focused on skill
development, value addition and marketing of forest-based products like
turmeric and honey;
(c) the details of the financial allocation made and the existing as well as future
plans for improving the supply chain, market linkages and infrastructure for
Kandhamal’s tribal produce along with the monitoring mechanisms in place to
evaluate progress thereof; and
(d) whether the Government proposes to provide special support for establishing
a Tribal Excellency Center in Kandhamal to enhance research, training and
innovation in tribal products and entrepreneurship and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a) to (d): Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development Federation of India Ltd.
(TRIFED) is implementing “Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Vikas Mission” (PMJVM)
scheme of Ministry of Tribal Affairs for socio-economic development of tribal
communities across the country including Kandhamal, Odisha. Under the
scheme, empanelment of tribal artisan and procurement of various tribal
products from them is a core activity for generating livelihood opportunities for
tribal communities. To achieve the same, TRIFED undertakes retail marketing of
tribal products through TRIBES India Outlets, E-Commerce and Exhibitions. So
03.04.2025 1052
far, 130 tribal suppliers / producers have been empaneled by TRIFED from the
State of Odisha.
Besides, financial assistance is also provided to State Implementing
Agencies (SIAs) for establishment of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) for
value addition of Minor Forest Produce (MFP) / non-MFPs. Additionally,
revolving funds are provided to State Governments for the procurement of 87
enlisted MFP items at Minimum Support Price (MSP). As on 28.02.2025,
TRIFED has sanctioned an amount of ₹240.00 lakhs for establishment of 16
VDVKs associating 4,560 beneficiaries in the State of Odisha. Additionally,
during financial year 2024-2025, TRIFED under B2B collaboration with ITC
facilitated sale of 15 MT of Turmeric valuing Rs. 26.25 lakhs by Pragati VDVK,
Daringbadi, Kandhamal to ITC.
UTILIZATION OF LAND OWNED BY PUBLIC SECTOR OIL COMPANIES
5482. SHRI N. K. PREMACHANDRAN:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has prepared a master plan for the utilization of
land owned by HPCL, if so, the action taken thereon;
(b) whether the Government has examined the proposal received from the public
representative for the utilization of land owned by HPCL at Edapallycotta,
Kollam, if so, the details of action taken thereon and the current status thereof;
(c) whether the Government has initiated action to protect the land owned by
HPCL at Edapallycotta, if so, the details thereof;
03.04.2025 1053
(d) the details regarding the planning of HPCL for utilizing the land owned by
HPCL at Edapallycotta;
(e) whether the Government proposes to utilize HPCL land for opening a new
venture at Edapallycotta, if so, the details thereof; and
(f) whether the Government proposes to utilize the land owned by the public
sector oil companies and if so, the action taken thereon?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (e): Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) is a Maharatna
Public Sector Undertaking (PSU). It is a Board driven Commercial entity which is
publicly listed. As an entity of this nature, it enjoys operational freedom in its day-
to-day commercial decisions. HPCL has informed that its land at Edapallycotta,
Kerala is secured by a boundary wall around it and security personnel have
been deployed at the plot for safeguarding it. HPCL has informed that presently,
there is no approved plan for utilization of the land for a new venture.
(f): No, as such decision is taken by the company concerned.

BENEFICIARIES UNDER PRADHAN MANTRI UJJWALA YOJANA
5484. SUSHRI BANSURI SWARAJ:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) the total number of beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
(PMUY) till February, 2025;
(b) the number of LPG refills delivered to the PMUY households till February,
2025;
(c) the number of LPG refills delivered on a daily basis to the PMUY households
in 2024; and
(d) the increase in per-capita consumption of PMUY beneficiaries from 2019-20
to February, 2025?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (d): Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched in May, 2016
with an objective to provide deposit free LPG connection to adult women from
poor households across the country. The target to release 8 crore connections
under PMUY was achieved in September 2019. To cover the remaining poor
households, Ujjwala 2.0 was launched in August 2021 with a target to release 1
03.04.2025 1060
crore additional PMUY connections, which was achieved in January 2022.
Subsequently, the Government decided to release 60 lakh more LPG
connections under Ujjwala 2.0 and the target of 1.60 crore Ujjwala 2.0
connections was also achieved during December 2022. Further, Government
approved release of additional 75 lakh connections under PMUY Scheme for the
period FY 2023-24 to 2025-26 which has already been achieved during July
2024. As on 01.03.2025, there are 10.33 crore PMUY connections across the
country.
Since launch of PMUY Scheme, OMCs have delivered a total of 234.02
crore LPG refills (in terms of 14.2 Kg cylinders) to PMUY customers, including
the initial installation refill, up to February 2025. During FY 2024-25 (till February
2025), OMCs have been delivering approximately 12.6 lakh LPG refills for
PMUY (in terms of 14.2 Kg cylinders) per day.
Further, as a result of various steps taken by Government to improve
access and affordability of domestic LPG for PMUY consumers, per capita
consumption of PMUY beneficiaries (in terms of no. of 14.2 kg LPG cylinders
taken per year) has increased from 3.01 (FY 2019-20) to 3.95 in FY 2023-24,
and 4.43 in FY 2024-25 (as on 01.03. 2025).
LOWER PANZARA IRRIGATION PROJECT
5485. DR. BACHHAV SHOBHA DINESH:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the irrigation projects on which work is under progress under
the Centrally Sponsored Schemes in Maharashtra along with the estimated cost
03.04.2025 1061
involved therein including the Central and State share prescribed for the
purpose, location-wise;
(b) the details of the Lower Panzara Irrigation Project including funds
sanctioned, released and utilized along with land acquisition status, current
status of implementation and expected timeline for completion of the project;
(c) whether there have been any delays in the implementation of the Panzara
Irrigation Project, if so, the reasons therefor;
(d) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to address these delays and
expedite the completion of the project;
(e) whether the Government has received any proposals from the State
Government of Maharashtra for irrigation in Dhule during the last five years and
the current year; and
(f) if so, the details thereof and the action taken by the Union Government
thereon?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a): There are two Centrally Sponsored Schemes (CSS) under implementation
for irrigation projects in Maharashtra. Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojna
(PMKSY) and Special Package scheme for completion of irrigation projects to
address agrarian distress in Vidarbha and Marathwada and other chronically
drought prone areas of rest of Maharashtra (Maharashtra Package). Further, two
components of PMKSY namely Accelerated Irrigation Benefits Programme
(AIBP) and Command Area Development and Water Management (CADand
WM) are under implementation in Maharashtra.
03.04.2025 1062
Details of irrigation projects along with their approved estimated cost,
eligible central assistance and State share along with districts benefitted are
given at Statement.
(b) to (d): Lower Panzara Irrigation Project was included under AIBP during
2009-10 and subsequently brought under the umbrella of PMKSY-AIBP and
CADand WM during 2015-16. Both the components of the project have been
declared as completed by Government of Maharashtra. Central assistance
provided to the project is Rs. 133.49 crore under AIBP and Rs. 8.64 crore under
CADand WM.
(e) and (f): Sulwade Jamphal Kanoli Project, benefitting Dhule district is being
implemented under Maharashtra Package.

SUPER SWACHH LEAGUE CATEGORY IN SWACHH SURVEKSHAN 2025
5486. DR. MALLU RAVI:
Will the Minister for HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has introduced the Super Swachh League category
to recognise top-performing cities in Swachh Survekshan; and
(b) if so, the details thereof including the criteria for inclusion, the names of cities
under each population category and the assessment process for retaining
league membership?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) and (b) : The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) launched the
toolkit for Swachh Survekshan, 2024 with the theme ‘Reduce Reuse Recycle’ on
17th January, 2025. In this edition of Swachh Survekshan a new feature “Super
Swachh League” is introduced to recognize cities that demonstrate extraordinary
performance based on their previous Swachh Survekshan Ranking (SS21, SS22
and SS23) have become a part of this league for the current year.
“Super Swachh League” includes the top ranked cities of their respective
population categories. These cities have been ranked as Top 3 in at least 2 of
the Swachh Survekshan editions conducted in the year 2021, 2022, and 2023.
These cities will now be assessed on an additional set of aspirational
indicators to remain in this special category of high-performing cities. The
ULB should score at least 85% in Swachh Survekshan to retain their position in
03.04.2025 1068
the league for subsequent years. List of Cities in the “Super Swachh League”
at present is enclosed as Statement.

COSTS INVOLVED IN THE PRODUCTION OF PETROLEUM AND NATURAL
GAS
5487. DR. M. K. VISHNU PRASAD:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
03.04.2025 1069
(a) the costs involved in the production of petroleum and natural gas from the
point of extraction of crude oil in off-shore/other countries till it is sold to the
consumers;
(b) the steps taken by the Government to reduce these costs so as to reduce the
costs of the products for the consumers; and
(c) the manner in which the Government has been successful in reducing such
costs so far?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) to (c): In the Oil and Gas industry various costs are involved till the sale of
final products to the consumers. From the point of extraction these inter-alia
include cost of making the extracted commodity merchantable, cost of
Importation/Transportation, costs of refining, marketing margins, statutory levies
etc.
The Government / Oil and Gas Companies have implemented several
measures to optimize fuel supply costs, focusing on efficiency improvements,
logistical streamlining, and fiscal interventions.
Cost reductions have been achieved through technological advancements
in refining and transportation.The rationalization of freight charges by PSU Oil
Marketing Companies (OMCs) has significantly reduced transportation costs,
narrowing the price gap between urban and remote areas. The expansion of the
City Gas Distribution (CGD) network has further improved fuel accessibility.
03.04.2025 1070
Government also took several other steps to insulate common citizens
from high international prices, which included diversifying the crude import
basket, invoking the provisions of Universal Service Obligation to ensure
availability of petrol and diesel in domestic market, increasing the blending of
ethanol in petrol, etc.
Fiscal interventions, such as excise duty reductions in 2022 and strategic
reductions in VAT by multiple states, have provided direct relief to consumers.
Prices of petrol and diesel are market determined and Public Sector Oil
Marketing Companies (OMCs) take appropriate decision on pricing of petrol and
diesel. In March, 2024, OMCs reduced the retail prices of petrol and diesel by
Rs. 2 per litre each.
India has been the only major economy in the world where the prices of
petrol and diesel have come down in recent years. Changes in prices of petrol
and diesel in some major economies between November 2021 and January
2025 are as under:
% age Change in Prices between Nov-21 and Jan-25
Country Petrol Diesel
India (Delhi) -13.60% -10.92%
France 14.21% 15.08%
Germany 7.87% 12.43%
Italy 8.65% 11.39%
Spain 8.67% 12.93%
UK 0.08% 2.61%
03.04.2025 1071
Canada 10.52% 23.05%
USA 4.83% 12.86%
Source: Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC)
Globally, Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) is the biggest program
of its kind that provides Domestic LPG to more than 100 million poor households
at an effective price of just about Rs. 35/Kg. Further, the effective price of
domestic LPG cylinder in neighbouring countries as on 01.01.2025 is as below:
Country Domestic LPG (Rs./14.2 kg.cyl.)
India 503.00*
Pakistan 1094.83
Sri Lanka 1231.53
Nepal 1206.65
Source: Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC)
*Effective cost to PMUY beneficiaries in Delhi, effective price is Rs. 803 for non-PMUY
consumers
The price of Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) in any location is fixed by
the City Gas Distribution (CGD) entity authorized by Petroleum and Natural Gas
Regulatory Board (PNGRB) after considering cost of gas procured, state taxes,
tariff and other components.
In order to minimise the impact of rising Natural Gas prices,the
Government has approved the revised domestic natural gas pricing guidelines
vide Gazette Notification dated 07.04.2023 for gas produced from nomination
03.04.2025 1072
fields of Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC)/ Oil India Limited (OIL), New
Exploration Licensing Policy (NELP) blocks, and pre-NELP blocks, where the
Production Sharing Contracts (PSCs) provide for Government approval of
prices. Under the revised guidelines, the price of such natural gas is determined
as 10% of the monthly average of the Indian Crude Basket and is notified on a
monthly basis. For gas produced by ONGC and OIL from their nomination
blocks, the Administered Price Mechanism (APM) price is subject to a floor of
$4.0/Metric Million British Thermal Unit (MMBTU) and a ceiling of $6.5/MMBTU.
This has led to price stability for consumers.

MAINTENANCE OF BRIDGES
5489. SHRIMATI PRATIMA MONDAL:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
the details of the initiatives being introduced to modernize and maintain ageing
bridges and flyovers for ensuring structural integrity and safety?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
The Government in the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways follows
a well established system of inspection / maintenance protocol for periodic
assessment of physical condition of bridges. Guidelines have been issued for
03.04.2025 1077
identification and rectification of bridges on National Highways which are in
severely damaged and precarious condition. A research and development
scheme on Bridge Health Monitoring System has been entrusted to IIT Madras
for structural health analysis of bridges. Guidelines have been issued on
4.3.2024 for real time Bridge Health Monitoring using sensors. Consultants are
deployed to monitor the status of bridges through online platform, Indian Bridge
Management System (IBMS).
SAFETY AND MAINTENANCE OF ROADS IN DHUBRI
5490. MD. RAKIBUL HUSSAIN:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the steps being taken to enhance road safety measures on National
Highways in Dhubri, including the installation of traffic signals, signages and
accident-prone zone identification sign boards;
(b) the manner in which the Government is ensuring the timely maintenance and
repair of roads in Dhubri especially during and after the monsoon season when
road conditions often worsen due to heavy rains; and
(c) whether there is any focus on improving the infrastructure for public transport,
such as the construction of bus terminals and safe passenger waiting areas, to
enhance travel experience for both locals and tourists in Dhubri, if so, the details
thereof?
03.04.2025 1078
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a): The steps taken to enhance road safety on National Highways in Dhubri
district of Assam State include:
i) Installation of reflective and standardized road safety signages for speed limits,
sharp curves, junctions,etc.,to enhance visibility and ensure safer driving
conditions.
ii) Identification and rectification of accident prone area (s)/Black Spot (s).
iii) Marking of center and edge lines with reflective studs and delineators to
enhance night-time visibility and ensure safer navigation for drivers.
iv) Installation of rumble strips, transverse bar marking, cat-eye reflectors and
guardrails/crash barriers at high-risk locations to enhance safety and prevent
accidents.
v) Road safety campaigns and stricter enforcement of traffic rules through
collaboration with law enforcement agencies.
vi) Installation of warning boards and cautionary signages.
Further, all safety norms have been incorporated right from design stage
to implementation stage on National Highways in Dhubri District. Proper
signages have also been provisioned as per Indian Road Congress (IRC):119-
2015 (Guidelines for Traffic Safety Barriers), and IRC:67-2012 (Code of Practice
for Road Signs).
03.04.2025 1079
Furthermore, the Government in the Ministry of Road Transport and
Highways has formulated a multi-pronged strategy to address the issue of road
safety based on Education, Engineering (both of roads and vehicles),
Enforcement and Emergency Care. Accordingly, various initiatives have been
taken for road safety in the country as per Statement.
(b): The standards and benchmarks for the maintenance of National Highways
are defined by the Indian Roads Congress (IRC) specifications and the
Government guidelines. These standards cover various aspects such as
pavement quality, road markings, signages, drainage systems and safety
features to ensure smooth and safe travel for users. The standards are reviewed
periodically based on advancements in technology, climatic conditions, traffic
growth and stakeholders' feedback.
(c): All 4-laning projects entrusted to National Highways and Infrastructure
Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL) in Dhubri District of Assam State
have provisions of Bus Bays and Truck Lay byes. Details are given below :
Sl. Bus bay with passenger Truck Lay Bye (in
Project
No. shelter (in Dhubri) Dhubri)

i. 3 1

ii. 4 0

NH-17
03.04.2025 1080
STATEMENT
Details of various initiatives taken by the Government in Ministry of Road
Transport and Highways for road safety : -
(1) Education:
i. Administers Road Safety Advocacy Scheme to provide financial assistance to
various agencies for raising awareness about road safety and for administering
road safety programs.
ii. Observance of National Road Safety Month/Week every year for spreading
awareness and strengthening road safety.
iii. Administers a scheme for setting up of Institutes of Driving Training and
Research (IDTRs), Regional Driving Training Centres (RDTCs) and Driving
Training Centres (DTCs) at state/district level across the Country.
(2) Engineering :
2.1. Road engineering:
i. Road Safety Audit (RSA) of all National Highways (NHs) has been made
mandatory through third party auditors/ experts at all stages i.e. design,
construction, operation and maintenance etc.
ii. High priority is accorded to identification and rectification of black spots
/accident spots on NHs.
iii. Road Safety Officer (RSO) has been designated at each Regional Office of
road owning agencies to look after RSA and other road safety related works.
03.04.2025 1081
iv. Administers the electronic Detailed Accident Report (e-DAR) Project to
establish a central repository for reporting, management and analysis of road
accidents data across the Country.
v. Issued guidelines for the provision of signages on Expressways and National
Highways to offer improved visibility and intuitive guidance to the drivers.
vi. Provisions have been made in the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 for failure to
comply with standards for the road design, construction and maintenance, as
prescribed by the Government from time to time.
2.2 Vehicle engineering:
Various initiatives were undertaken to make vehicles safer, including the
following:-
i. Mandatory provision of an airbag for the passenger seated on the front seat of
a vehicle, next to the driver.
ii. Prescribed norms related to safety measures for children below four years of
age, riding or being carried on a motor cycle. It also specifies use of a safety
harness, crash helmet and restricts speed to 40kmph.
iii. Mandatory provisions for fitment of following listed safety technologies: -
For M1 category vehicles:
Seat Belt Reminder (SBR) for driver and co-driver.

Manual Override for central locking system

Over speed warning system.

03.04.2025 1082
For all M and N category vehicles:
Reverse Parking Alert System

iv. Mandated Anti-Lock Braking System (ABS) for certain classes of L [Motor
vehicle with less than four wheels and includes a Quadricycle], M [Motor
vehicles with at least four wheels used for carrying passengers] and N [Motor
vehicles with at least four wheels used for carrying goods which may also carry
persons in addition to goods, subject to conditions stipulated in BIS standards]
categories.
v. Mandated speed limiting function/speed limiting device in all transport
vehicles, except for two wheelers, three wheelers, quadricycles, fire tenders,
ambulances and police vehicles.
vi. Published the rules for recognition, regulation and control of Automated
Testing Stations (ATSs), which define the procedure for fitness testing of
vehicles through automated equipment and the procedure for grant of fitness
certificate by ATSs. The rules have been further amended on 31.10.2022 and
14.03.2024.
vii. Formulated the Vehicle Scrapping Policy based on incentives/dis-incentives
and for creating an ecosystem to phase out old, unfit and polluting vehicles.
viii. Formulated a Scheme to set up one model Inspection and Certification
Centre in each State/UT for testing the fitness of vehicles through an automated
system.
03.04.2025 1083
ix. Published rules regarding the Bharat New Car Assessment Program
(BNCAP) to introduce the concept of safety rating of passenger cars and
empower consumers to take informed decisions.
x. Published rules regarding prescribed level playing field in the area of
manufacturing of buses by Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and Bus
Body Builders.
xi. Mandated vehicles, manufactured on or after 1st October, 2025, shall be
fitted with an air-conditioning system for the cabin of vehicles of N2 (goods
vehicle with gross vehicle weight exceeding 3.5 tonnes but not exceeding 12.0
tonnes) and N3 (goods vehicle with gross vehicle weight exceeding 12.0 tonnes)
category.
xii. Published rules for revision of standards for Safety Belt, Restraint Systems
and Safety Belt Reminder to provide provisions for applicability of revised
standards for safety belt assemblies, safety belt anchorages and the installation
of Safety Belts and Restraint Systems, in Motor vehicles of category M,N and L7
w.e.f. 01st April, 2025. Further, the vehicles of category M1, manufactured on
and after the 1st April 2025, shall meet the requirement of safety belt reminder
for all front facing rear seats as per AIS-145-2018.
(3) Enforcement:
i. The Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019 as stands implemented provides
for strict penalties for ensuring compliance and enhancing deterrence for
violation of traffic rules and strict enforcement through use of technology. Traffic
03.04.2025 1084
management and enforcement is essentially in the domain of State
Governments/UT Administrations. While Union Government formulates rules
under the Motor Vehicle Act, 1988, the enforcement of these rules comes under
the purview of State Governments/UT Administrations. The Government has
specifically requested all States/ UTs for strict implementation of provisions of
Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 related to overloading of vehicles.
ii. Issued rules for Electronic Monitoring and Enforcement of Road Safety. The
rules specify the detailed provisions for placement of electronic enforcement
devices on high risk and high density corridors on National Highways, State
Highways and critical junctions cities having a population of more than one
million in the country and cities under National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
iii. On 10th June, 2024, the Government has issued an advisory to all the States
and Union territories on technological interventions for ensuring compliance with
Motor Vehicle Act, 1988.
(4) Emergency care:
i. Published rules for the protection of Good Samaritan, who in good faith,
voluntarily and without expectation of any reward or compensation renders
emergency medical or non-medical care or assistance at the scene of an
accident to the victim or transports such victim to the hospital.
ii. Enhanced compensation of victims of Hit and Run motor accidents (from Rs.
12,500 to Rs. 50,000 for grievous hurt and from Rs. 25,000 to Rs.2,00,000 for
death).
03.04.2025 1085
iii. The National Highways Authority of India has made provisions for
ambulances with paramedical staff/Emergency Medical Technician/Nurse at toll
plazas on the completed corridors of National Highways.
iv. Government in Ministry of Road Transport and Highways along with National
Health Authority (NHA), has implemented a pilot program for providing cashless
treatment to victims of road accidents in Chandigarh, Haryana, Punjab,
Uttarakhand, Puducherry and Assam.
